title.zh: 卷三 肅宗孝章帝紀
title.en: Volume 3: Annals of Emperor Suzong Xiaozhang
title.raw: 卷三 肅宗孝章帝紀 Volume 3: Annals of Emperor Suzong Xiaozhang

sentences.zh: 肅宗孝章皇帝諱炟，顯宗第五子也。

translations.literal: The Emperor with the temple name Suzong and the posthumous title Xiaozhang, whose tabooed personal name was Da, was the fifth son of Emperor Xianzong.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang—temple name Suzong, posthumous epithet Xiaozhang—was Liu Da, the fifth son of Emperor Ming (Xianzong).

sentences.zh: （《謚法》曰：「溫克令儀曰章。

translations.literal: (The 《Posthumous Title Laws》 states: "One who is mild, self-restrained, and exemplary in deportment is called Zhang (Brilliant).
translations.idiomatic: (The regulations on posthumous names say: "Gentle, self-controlled, and dignified in bearing" earns the epithet Zhang.

sentences.zh: 」《伏侯古今注》曰：「炟之字曰著，音丁達反。

translations.literal: " The 《Fu Hou Notes Ancient and Modern》 states: "The courtesy name of Da was Zhu; its sound is ding-da fan.)
translations.idiomatic: The 《Fu Hou Commentary》 gives his style as Zhu, read with the ding initial and da rime.)

sentences.zh: 」）母賈貴人。

translations.literal: ") His mother was the honorable lady Jia.
translations.idiomatic: His mother was Lady Jia, mother of the heir.

sentences.zh: 永平三年，立爲皇太子。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yongping, he was established as heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 3 he was named crown prince.

sentences.zh: 少寬容，好儒術，顯宗器重之。

translations.literal: In youth he was tolerant and magnanimous; he loved Confucian learning; Emperor Xianzong valued him highly.
translations.idiomatic: From boyhood he was easygoing and fond of Confucian scholarship; Emperor Ming thought the world of him.

sentences.zh: 十八年八月壬子，即皇帝位，年十九。

translations.literal: On the day renzi in the eighth month of the eighteenth year, he assumed the imperial throne; he was nineteen sui.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi in the eighth month of Yongping 18 he ascended the throne at nineteen.

sentences.zh: 尊皇后曰皇太后。

translations.literal: The empress was honored as empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: The empress was elevated to empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 壬戌，葬孝明皇帝于顯節陵。

translations.literal: On the day renxu, Emperor Xiaoming was buried at Xianjie Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: On renxu Emperor Ming was interred at the Xianjie tomb.

sentences.zh: （《帝王紀》曰：「顯節陵方三百步，高八丈。

translations.literal: (The 《Record of Emperors and Kings》 states: "Xianjie Mausoleum measured three hundred paces square and was eight zhang high.
translations.idiomatic: (The 《Imperial Annals》 says the Xianjie mound was three hundred paces on a side and eight zhang high.

sentences.zh: 其地故富壽亭也，西北去洛陽三十七里。

translations.literal: Its ground was the former Fushou pavilion; northwest from Luoyang thirty-seven li.
translations.idiomatic: The site was the old Fushou post station, thirty-seven li northwest of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 」） 冬十月丁未，大赦天下。

translations.literal: ") In winter, in the tenth month, on the day dingwei, there was a great amnesty of the empire.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter, on dingwei, the court proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 賜民爵，人二級，爲父後及孝悌、力田人三級，脫無名數及流人欲占者人一級，爵過公乘得移與子若同產子；

translations.literal: The people were granted noble ranks, two grades per person; to those who succeeded their fathers and to the exemplars of filial piety and fraternal duty and the champions of diligent farming, three grades per person; to those who cast off unregistered status and to vagrants who wished to register, one grade per person; where rank exceeded gongcheng, it might be transferred to a son or to a full brother's son;
translations.idiomatic: Commoners received two steps of noble rank, three for filial exemplars and chief farmers, one for those who registered formerly uncounted persons or vagrants; excess rank above gongcheng could pass to a son or brother's son;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤𤸇、貧不能自存者粟，人三斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the solitary, the seriously ill, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: three hu of grain for each widower, widow, orphan, person living alone, the gravely disabled, and the destitute.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：「朕以眇身，託于王侯之上，統理萬機，懼失厥中，兢兢業業，未知所濟。

translations.literal: An edict stated: "I, in my slight person, have been entrusted above the kings and marquels; I oversee and manage the myriad threads of state; I fear missing the mean; fearful and trembling, I do not know how to succeed.
translations.idiomatic: An edict read: "Young as I am, I sit above the princes yet must steer every affair of state; I fear losing the middle way and do not know how I shall manage.

sentences.zh: 深惟守文之主，必建師傅之官。

translations.literal: Deeply I reflect that a ruler who preserves the pattern of culture must establish the office of tutor and protector.
translations.idiomatic: A sovereign who keeps the civil peace must have true grand tutors.

sentences.zh: 詩不云乎：『不愆不忘，率由舊章。

translations.literal: Does not the 《Odes》 say: 'Do not err, do not forget; follow the ancient statutes.
translations.idiomatic: The 《Odes》 ask: 'Forget no lesson, stray from no ancient rule.

sentences.zh: 』（詩大雅也。

translations.literal: ' (This is text from the "Sheng Min" ode in the Major Odes.)
translations.idiomatic: ' (The line comes from the Major Odes.)

sentences.zh: 鄭玄云：「愆，過也。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan explains: "Qian means fault.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan glosses: "Qian is transgression.

sentences.zh: 率，循也。

translations.literal: Shuai means follow.
translations.idiomatic: Shuai means to follow.

sentences.zh: 由，用也。

translations.literal: You means use.
translations.idiomatic: You means 'to use.'

sentences.zh: 言成王之令德，不過誤，不違失，皆循用舊典文章，謂周公之禮法。

translations.literal: It speaks of King Cheng's excellent virtue: he did not err, he did not stray; he all followed and used the old canons and patterning—that is, the ritual law of the Duke of Zhou."
translations.idiomatic: It praises King Cheng for erring neither in word nor deed and for holding to the Duke of Zhou's ritual code."

sentences.zh: 」）行太尉事節鄉侯憙三世在位，爲國元老；

translations.literal: ") Marquis of Jiexiang Xi, acting as grand commandant, has held office through three reigns; he is the elder statesman of the state;
translations.idiomatic: Marquis of Jiexiang Zhao Xi has served as acting grand commandant across three reigns—he is the elder statesman of the court;

sentences.zh: （趙憙，光武時爲太尉，明帝時行太尉事，故曰三代在位。

translations.literal: (Zhao Xi was grand commandant under Guangwu and under Emperor Ming acted as grand commandant—hence it says "three generations in office."
translations.idiomatic: (Zhao Xi was grand commandant under Guangwu and acted as grand commandant under Ming, hence "three reigns at the center."

sentences.zh: 元，長也。

translations.literal: Yuan means senior.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan here means "senior."

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：「方叔元老。

translations.literal: The 《Odes》 says: "Fang Shu is the elder statesman."
translations.idiomatic: The 《Odes》 call Fang Shu "the old pillar of state."

sentences.zh: 」）司空融典職六年，（融，牟融。

translations.literal: ") Minister of works Rong has held his office six years. (Rong is Mou Rong.
translations.idiomatic: Minister of Works Mou Rong has served six years. (Rong is Mou Rong.

sentences.zh: ）勤勞不怠。

translations.literal: ) Diligent in toil, never slackening.
translations.idiomatic: ) He has labored without slackening.

sentences.zh: 其以憙爲太傅，融爲太尉，並錄尚書事。

translations.literal: Therefore appoint Xi as grand tutor and Rong as grand commandant, and let them both concurrently supervise the masters of writing.
translations.idiomatic: Appoint Xi grand tutor and Rong grand commandant, both with concurrent authority over the masters of writing.

sentences.zh: （武帝初以張子孺領尚書事。

translations.literal: (At the beginning of Emperor Wu, Zhang Ziru held concurrent charge of the masters of writing.
translations.idiomatic: (Emperor Wu first had Zhang Ziru "hold" the masters of writing.

sentences.zh: 錄尚書事由此始。

translations.literal: From this began the concurrent supervision of the masters of writing.
translations.idiomatic: Thus began the title "supervisor of the masters of writing."

sentences.zh: ）『三事大夫，莫肯夙夜』，小雅之所傷也。

translations.literal: ") 'The three affairs' great ministers are unwilling to labor early and late'—this is what the "Minor Odes" lament.
translations.idiomatic: The 《Odes》 also mourn that "the three high ministers will not toil day and night"—

sentences.zh: （詩雨無正之文也。

translations.literal: (This is text from "Yu wu zheng" in the Odes.
translations.idiomatic: (from "Yu wu zheng" in the Odes;

sentences.zh: 三事，三公也。

translations.literal: San shi means the three dukes.)
translations.idiomatic: san shi means the three dukes.)

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注云：「幽王在外，三公及諸侯隨而行者，皆無復君臣之禮，不肯晨夜省王。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan's commentary explains: "When King You was abroad, the three dukes and the various marquels who followed in traveling no longer observed the rites of lord and minister and were unwilling morning and night to attend the king.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan explains that when King You fled, the three dukes and marquels who followed him forgot lord-and-minister duty and would not attend him morning or night.

sentences.zh: 」）『予違汝弼，汝無靣從』，（尚書益稷之文也。

translations.literal: "") 'If I stray, correct me; do not agree to my face'—(this is text from "Yi and Ji" in the 《Documents》.
translations.idiomatic: "If I miss the Way, set me right; do not merely nod"—from the 《Documents》, "Yi and Ji",

sentences.zh: 孔安國注云：「我違道，汝當以義輔正我，無面從我。

translations.literal: Kong Anguo's commentary explains: "If I stray from the Way, you ought with righteousness to correct me; do not agree to my face.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Anguo glosses: when the ruler errs, ministers must correct him with right principle, not flatter to his face.

sentences.zh: 」）股肱之正義也。

translations.literal: "") This is the correct meaning of thigh and arm [ministers].
translations.idiomatic: That is the true duty of arm-and-thigh ministers.

sentences.zh: 羣后百僚勉思厥職，各貢忠誠，以輔不逮。

translations.literal: You host of lords and hundred officials, strive to ponder your duties; each offer loyal sincerity, in order to assist where I fall short.
translations.idiomatic: All you lords and officials: perfect your offices, speak in good faith, and help me where I fail.

sentences.zh: 申勑四方，稱朕意焉。

translations.literal: Repeatedly admonish the four quarters and accord with my intent."
translations.idiomatic: Send my meaning to every quarter of the realm."

sentences.zh: 」 十一月戊戌，蜀郡太守第五倫爲司空。

translations.literal: " In the eleventh month, on the day wuxu, Di Wu Lun of Shujun was made minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month, on wuxu, Di Wu Lun, governor of Shujun, became minister of works.

sentences.zh: 詔征西將軍耿秉屯酒泉。

translations.literal: It was ordered that the general who smites the west, Geng Bing, encamp at Jiuquan.
translations.idiomatic: General Geng Bing who smites the west was ordered to garrison Jiuquan.

sentences.zh: （酒泉，今肅州縣也。

translations.literal: (Jiuquan is the present Su prefecture county.
translations.idiomatic: (Jiuquan is in modern Gansu.

sentences.zh: 《前書音義》曰：「城下有泉，其味若酒，因名酒泉焉。

translations.literal: The 《Former Han Treatise on Sounds and Meanings》 states: "Below the city wall is a spring whose taste is like wine; therefore it was named Jiuquan."
translations.idiomatic: The Han commentaries say a spring below the city tasted like wine, hence the name "Wine Spring."

sentences.zh: 」）遣酒泉太守段彭救戊己校尉耿恭。

translations.literal: ") Duan Peng, governor of Jiuquan, was sent to rescue the Wu-Ji colonel Geng Gong.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Duan Peng of Jiuquan was dispatched to relieve Colonel Geng Gong.

sentences.zh: 甲辰晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On the last day of the month jiachen, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: At month's end on jiachen the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 於是避正殿，寑兵，不聽事五日。

translations.literal: Thereupon he avoided the main hall, laid aside weapons, and did not hear cases for five days.
translations.idiomatic: He vacated the main hall, stood down the guards, and suspended court business for five days.

sentences.zh: 詔有司各上封事。

translations.literal: It was ordered that the responsible officials each submit sealed memorials.
translations.idiomatic: Every ministry was told to file sealed advice.

sentences.zh: 十二月癸巳，有司奏言：「孝明皇帝聖德淳茂，劬勞日𣅳，（日昃，日昳。

translations.literal: On the day guisi in the twelfth month, the responsible officials memorialized, saying: "Emperor Xiaoming's sage virtue was pure and luxuriant; he toiled until the sun slanted. (Ri ze means the sun past noon.
translations.idiomatic: On guisi in the twelfth month officials memorialized: "Emperor Ming's virtue was deep and rich; he worked past midday. (Ri ze is afternoon;

sentences.zh: 《尚書》曰：「文王自朝至于日中昃，不遑暇食。

translations.literal: The 《Documents》 states: "King Wen from morning until midday at the sun's slant had no leisure even to eat.
translations.idiomatic: the 《Documents》 say King Wen from dawn to mid-afternoon had no time to eat.

sentences.zh: 」）身御浣衣，食無兼珍。

translations.literal: ") He personally wore washed cloth; at meals there were no doubled delicacies.
translations.idiomatic: He wore patched silk and took no second dish at dinner.

sentences.zh: 澤臻四表，（《尚書》曰：「光被四表。

translations.literal: His grace reached the four outer limits. (The 《Documents》 states: "His light was shed to the four outer limits."
translations.idiomatic: His kindness spread to the four seas. (The 《Documents》 say "his light reached the four borders."

sentences.zh: 」）遠人慕化，僬僥、儋耳，款塞自至。

translations.literal: ") Distant people admired transformation; the Jiaojiao and Dan'er knocked at the passes and came of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Far peoples sought civilization; pygmies and Dan'er tribes came to the frontier gates unbidden.

sentences.zh: （款，扣。

translations.literal: (Kuan means to knock.
translations.idiomatic: (Kuan means "to knock at."

sentences.zh: 僬僥、儋耳解見明紀。

translations.literal: Explanations of Jiaojiao and Dan'er appear in the annals of Emperor Ming.)
translations.idiomatic: Jiaojiao and Dan'er are glossed in Emperor Ming's annals.)

sentences.zh: ）克伐鬼方，（鬼方，遠方。

translations.literal: He conquered and struck Gui Fang. (Gui Fang means a distant region.
translations.idiomatic: He chastised "Gui Fang" (a distant enemy.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「高宗伐鬼方，三年克之。

translations.literal: The 《Changes》 states: "Gaozong attacked Gui Fang; in three years he conquered them."
translations.idiomatic: The 《Book of Changes》 says Gaozong campaigned three years against Gui Fang and won.

sentences.zh: 」）開道西域，威靈廣被，無思不服。

translations.literal: ") He opened roads through the Western Regions; awesome numen spread broadly; none failed to submit who did not think of it.
translations.idiomatic: He opened the Western Regions so that Han majesty reached everywhere and none could refuse allegiance.

sentences.zh: 以烝庶爲憂，不以天下爲樂。

translations.literal: He took the multitude of commoners as his care, not taking the empire as his pleasure.
translations.idiomatic: He worried for the common people, not for his own delight in ruling.

sentences.zh: 備三雍之敎，躬養老之禮。

translations.literal: He prepared the instruction of the three Yong halls; personally he performed the rite of caring for the aged.
translations.idiomatic: He perfected the three suburban academies and personally celebrated the old-age rites.

sentences.zh: 作登歌，正予樂，愽貫六蓺，（周禮保氏敎之六蓺：一曰禮，二曰樂，三曰射，四曰馭，五曰書，六曰數。

translations.literal: He made ascendant hymns, corrected my music, and broadly mastered the six arts. (The 《Zhou Rituals》 director of the exterior teaches the six arts: first ritual, second music, third archery, fourth charioteering, fifth writing, sixth reckoning.
translations.idiomatic: He set court hymns to rights, reformed the imperial music, and mastered the six arts. (The 《Zhou li》 lists ritual, music, archery, charioteering, writing, and numbers;

sentences.zh: 《前書》蓺文志曰以禮、樂、春秋、易、詩、書爲六蓺。

translations.literal: The 《Former Han Treatise on Arts and Letters》 takes ritual, music, the Spring and Autumn Annals, the 《Changes》, the 《Odes》, and the 《Documents》 as the six arts.
translations.idiomatic: the Han "Bibliographic Treatise" counts the six classics as the six arts.

sentences.zh: 博貫謂究極深幽耳。

translations.literal: "Broadly mastered" means he exhaustively penetrated the profound and recondite.)
translations.idiomatic: "Broad mastery" means he plumbed their depths.)

sentences.zh: ）不舍晝夜。

translations.literal: He did not slack day or night.
translations.idiomatic: He never flagged night or day.

sentences.zh: 聦明淵塞，著在圖讖。

translations.literal: Keen of hearing and clear-sighted, profound and blocking [faults]—this appears in the charts and prognostications.
translations.idiomatic: His acumen and depth were foretold in apocryphal charts.

sentences.zh: （河圖曰：「圖出代，九天開明，受用嗣興，十代以光。

translations.literal: (The 《River Chart》 states: "When the chart emerges in place of the dynasty, the nine heavens open brightness; receiving and using, the succession rises; ten generations thereby shine.
translations.idiomatic: (The 《River Chart》 promises nine heavens opened and ten generations of glory.

sentences.zh: 」又括地象曰：「十代禮樂，文雅並出。

translations.literal: " Moreover the 《Comprehending the Earth in Images》 states: "Ten generations of ritual and music; refinement and pattern issue together.
translations.idiomatic: The 《Kuodi xiang》 says ten reigns of ritual and culture will follow.

sentences.zh: 」謂明帝也。

translations.literal: " This refers to Emperor Ming.)
translations.idiomatic: Both allude to Emperor Ming.)

sentences.zh: ）至德所感，通於神明。

translations.literal: What the utmost virtue touches reaches through to the spirits and gods.
translations.idiomatic: Such virtue moved even the spirits.

sentences.zh: 功烈光于四海，仁風行於千載。

translations.literal: Merit and blazing achievement shone to the four seas; humane wind walked a thousand ages.
translations.idiomatic: His deeds lit the four seas; his humane example will last a thousand years.

sentences.zh: 而深執謙謙，自稱不德，無起寑廟，埽地而祭，除日祀之法，（春秋外傳曰：「日祭，月祀，時享。

translations.literal: Yet he deeply held to humble yielding, calling himself lacking in virtue; he ordered no resting temple; sweep the ground and sacrifice; he abolished the method of daily sacrifice. (The 《Discourses of the States》 states: "Daily sacrifice, monthly offering, seasonal feast.
translations.idiomatic: Yet he called himself unworthy, forbade a separate temple, asked only for swept-earth offerings, and ended daily sacrifices. (The outer commentary says: daily, monthly, seasonal rites;

sentences.zh: 祖禰則日祭，高曾則月祀，三祧則時享。

translations.literal: For grandfather and father there is daily sacrifice; for high ancestor and great-great-grandfather there is monthly offering; for the three remote temples there is seasonal feast.
translations.idiomatic: near ancestors daily, remoter ones monthly, the three distant temples by season;

sentences.zh: 」今此除日祀之法，從時月之祭。

translations.literal: " Now in abolishing the method of daily sacrifice, he follows sacrifice by season and month.)
translations.idiomatic: his edict followed seasonal and monthly worship instead of daily.)

sentences.zh: ）省送終之禮，遂藏主於光烈皇后更衣別室。

translations.literal: He reduced the rites for sending off the end; thereupon the spirit tablet was deposited in the separate chamber for changing dress of Empress Guanglie.
translations.idiomatic: He cut funeral pomp and housed his tablet with Empress Guanglie in her robing chamber.

sentences.zh: 天下聞之，莫不悽愴。

translations.literal: When the empire heard this, none was not grieved and moved.
translations.idiomatic: The whole realm mourned when it heard.

sentences.zh: 陛下至孝烝烝，奉順聖德。

translations.literal: Your Majesty's utmost filial piety is warm and thick; you uphold and follow sage virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty's filial devotion is deep; you follow that sage example.

sentences.zh: 臣愚以爲更衣在中門之外，處所殊別，宜尊廟曰顯宗，其四時禘祫，於光武之堂，閒祀悉還更衣，（《續漢書》曰：「五年再殷祭，三年一祫，五年一禘。

translations.literal: Your servant foolishly considers that the robing chamber lies outside the middle gate in a place altogether separate; it is fitting to honor the temple with the name Xianzong; as for the seasonal di and xia offerings at Guangwu's hall, and the intervening sacrifices all return to the robing chamber. (The 《Continued Han Documents》 states: "Once every five years there is the grand Yin sacrifice; once every three years a xia; once every five years a di.
translations.idiomatic: We humbly note the robing hall stands outside the inner gate—separate from the main shrine. We ask that the dynastic temple name Xianzong be granted; grand di and xia should be offered in Guangwu's hall, minor seasonal rites in the robing hall. (The 《Xu Han shu》 gives the five-year Yin cycle: xia every three years, di every five;

sentences.zh: 父爲昭，南向；

translations.literal: The father is zhao, facing south;
translations.idiomatic: fathers face south as zhao;

sentences.zh: 子爲穆，北向。

translations.literal: the son is mu, facing north.
translations.idiomatic: sons face north as mu.

sentences.zh: 禘以夏四月，祫以冬十月。

translations.literal: The di is in summer, the fourth month; the xia is in winter, the tenth month.
translations.idiomatic: The di falls in the fourth month of summer, the xia in the tenth month of winter.

sentences.zh: 禘之爲言諦，諦審昭穆尊卑之義。

translations.literal: As for di, the word means "examine"—examining clearly the meaning of zhao and mu, senior and junior.
translations.idiomatic: Di means "to sort": it fixes zhao-mu seniority.

sentences.zh: 祫者，合也。

translations.literal: Xia means "joining."
translations.idiomatic: Xia means "joining" the lineage.

sentences.zh: 冬十月五穀成，故骨肉合飲食於祖廟，謂之殷祭。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, the five grains are complete; therefore flesh and bone join in drinking and eating at the ancestral temple—this is called the Yin sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month the harvest is in, so kin gather at the high temple for the great Yin feast.

sentences.zh: 四時正祭外，有五月甞麥，三伏立秋甞粢盛酎，十月甞稻等，謂之閒祀，即各于更衣之殿。

translations.literal: Outside the regular sacrifices of the four seasons, there are in the fifth month the tasting of the new wheat, at the three fu periods and at Establishment of Autumn the tasting of the new grain and the offering of strained ale, in the tenth month the tasting of the new rice—these are called intervening sacrifices, each performed in the robing hall.
translations.idiomatic: Besides the four seasonal rites come minor tastings—new wheat in the fifth month, new grain and autumn ale at the three fu and at Beginning of Autumn, new rice in the tenth month—each in the robing hall.

sentences.zh: 更衣者，非正處也。

translations.literal: The robing chamber is not the correct place.
translations.idiomatic: The robing hall is not the principal shrine.

sentences.zh: 園中有寑，有便殿。

translations.literal: Within the mausoleum park there is a resting hall and a convenience hall.
translations.idiomatic: The tomb precinct has a main hall and side halls.

sentences.zh: 寑者，陵上正殿。

translations.literal: The resting hall is the correct hall above the tomb.
translations.idiomatic: The "resting" hall is the chief structure on the mound.

sentences.zh: 便殿，寑側之別殿，即更衣也。

translations.literal: The convenience hall is a separate hall beside the resting hall—that is, the robing chamber.
translations.idiomatic: The "convenience" hall beside it is the robing chamber.

sentences.zh: 」）共進武德之舞，如孝文皇帝祫祭高廟故事。

translations.literal: ") Together advance the dance of 《Wu De》, according to the precedent when Emperor Xiaowen performed the xia sacrifice at the lofty temple."
translations.idiomatic: Present the 《Wu De》 dance as Emperor Wen once did at the high temple xia offering."

sentences.zh: 」（前書高廟奏武德、文始、五行之舞。

translations.literal: " (The 《Former Han》 at the lofty temple performed the dances of 《Wu De》, 《Wen Shi》, and 《Wu Xing》.
translations.idiomatic: (Former Han used 《Wu De》, 《Wen Shi》, and 《Wu Xing》 at Gaozu's temple.

sentences.zh: ）制曰：「可。

translations.literal: ") The rescript stated: "It may be so."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor answered: "Granted."

sentences.zh: 」 是歲，牛疫。

translations.literal: " That year, there was cattle plague.
translations.idiomatic: That year a cattle plague swept the north.

sentences.zh: 京師及三州大旱，詔勿收兖、豫、徐州田租、芻稾，其以見穀賑給貧人。

translations.literal: The capital region and three provinces suffered great drought; it was ordered not to collect field rent or fodder straw in Yan, Yu, and Xu provinces; grain on hand was to be used to relieve the poor.
translations.idiomatic: The capital and three provinces baked in drought; Yan, Yu, and Xu were excused rent and fodder tax, and stored grain was issued to the needy.

sentences.zh: 建初元年春正月，詔三州郡國：「方春東作，恐人稍受稟，（稟，給也。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianchu, in spring, in the first month, it was ordered to the commanderies and principalities of the three provinces: "As spring opens eastern labor, I fear people will gradually receive rations. (Bin means to supply.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 1, first month of spring: the three drought provinces were told: "Spring plowing has begun; I fear relief grain is coming too slowly. (Bin means "issue rations."

sentences.zh: 稍，爲少。

translations.literal: Shao means little.
translations.idiomatic: Shao means "little by little."

sentences.zh: 少給之不頓與。

translations.literal: To supply them little by little is not to give all at once.
translations.idiomatic: Do not dump the whole ration at once.

sentences.zh: ）徃來煩劇，或妨耕農。

translations.literal: ") Coming and going is vexatious and may hinder plowing and farming.
translations.idiomatic: Constant travel for pickup wastes the spring plow.

sentences.zh: 其各實覈尤貧者，計所貸并與之。

translations.literal: Let each verify the especially poor and, calculating what is lent, give it all together to them.
translations.idiomatic: Identify the poorest households and deliver their full loaned grain in one trip.

sentences.zh: （并音必政反。

translations.literal: (Bin here is read with the sound bi-zheng fan.)
translations.idiomatic: (Bin is read with the bi initial and zheng rime.)

sentences.zh: ）流人欲歸本者，郡縣其實稟，令足還到，聽過止官亭，無雇舍宿。

translations.literal: ") As for vagrants who wish to return to their native places, let the commanderies and counties in truth supply them so that they have enough to return; allow them to pass and stop at official post stations; do not hire private lodgings for the night."
translations.idiomatic: Vagrants heading home must receive enough grain from counties to complete the journey; let them rest at official hostels and not pay for private inns."

sentences.zh: 長吏親躬，無使貧弱遺脫，小吏豪右得容姦妄。

translations.literal: Let senior officials go in person; do not cause the poor and weak to be left out or omitted, or petty clerks and powerful families on the right to harbor wickedness and falsehood.
translations.idiomatic: Magistrates must go in person so the needy are not missed and clerks and magnates cannot hide fraud.

sentences.zh: （《前書》曰，百石已下有斗食佐史之秩，言小吏也。

translations.literal: (The Former Han states that below one hundred piculs there were the ranks of dou-shi assistant scribes, meaning petty clerks.
translations.idiomatic: (Former Han ranked petty clerks below one hundred piculs, the dou-shi assistants.

sentences.zh: ）詔書旣下，勿得稽留，刺史明加督察尤無狀者。

translations.literal: ) Once the edict has been issued, it must not be delayed or held up; let the regional inspectors clearly intensify supervision and inspection, especially of those who are utterly without good record.
translations.idiomatic: Once the edict goes out there must be no foot-dragging; inspectors shall watch especially the worst offenders.

sentences.zh: 」（無狀謂其罪惡尤大，其狀無可寄言，故云無狀。

translations.literal: " (Wu zhuang means that their crimes are especially great and their condition admits of no excuse in words; therefore it is called "without condition."
translations.idiomatic: "Wu zhuang" means crimes so grave they defy description.

sentences.zh: 它皆類此。

translations.literal: In other cases all are of this sort.)
translations.idiomatic: Other glosses follow the same rule.)

sentences.zh: ） 丙寅，詔曰：「比年牛多疾疫，墾田減少，穀價頗貴，人以流亡。

translations.literal: On the day bingyin, an edict stated: "In recent years oxen have often suffered plague; opened fields have decreased; grain prices have become rather expensive; people have fled and wandered.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin an edict said: "Cattle plague, shrinking tillage, dear grain, and drifting refugees have worn the realm down.

sentences.zh: 方春東作，宜及時務。

translations.literal: As spring opens eastern labor, it is fitting to catch up with seasonal tasks.
translations.idiomatic: Spring plowing is here; every county must keep pace with the season.

sentences.zh: 二千石勉勸農桑，弘致勞來。

translations.literal: Let those of two thousand piculs exert themselves to encourage farming and sericulture, broadly bring about labor and recruitment.
translations.idiomatic: Two-thousand-picul officials must push plowing and silkworms and welcome returning laborers.

sentences.zh: 羣公庶尹，各推精誠，專急人事。

translations.literal: You dukes and many yin, each push forth essential sincerity, concentrate urgently on human affairs.
translations.idiomatic: All high ministers must give honest effort and put the people first.

sentences.zh: 罪非殊死，須立秋案驗。

translations.literal: For crimes that are not execution by dismemberment, it is necessary to wait until Establishment of Autumn to examine the cases.
translations.idiomatic: No death sentence short of the gravest may be reviewed until autumn.

sentences.zh: 有司明慎選舉，進柔良，退貪猾，順時令，理冤獄。

translations.literal: Let the responsible officials clearly and carefully select and recommend; advance the mild and good, retire the greedy and crafty; comply with seasonal commands; set right wrongful imprisonment.
translations.idiomatic: Let officials choose men with care, promote the gentle, dismiss the corrupt, heed the seasons, and clear the jails.

sentences.zh: 『五敎在寬』，帝典所美；

translations.literal: "The five teachings lie in leniency" - this is what the Canon of the Emperor praises;
translations.idiomatic: "The five teachings rest on mercy" - so the sage kings taught;

sentences.zh: （五敎謂父義、母慈、兄友、弟恭、子孝也。

translations.literal: (The five teachings mean father righteous, mother kind, elder brother friendly, younger brother respectful, son filial.
translations.idiomatic: (The five bonds: righteous fathers, kind mothers, friendly elder brothers, respectful younger brothers, filial sons.

sentences.zh: 尚書舜典曰：「汝作司徒，敬敷五敎在寬。

translations.literal: The Canon of Shun in the Documents states: "You shall be minister of education, reverently spreading the five teachings in leniency."
translations.idiomatic: The Canon of Shun says the minister of education must spread those five teachings with mercy.

sentences.zh: 」）『愷悌君子』，大雅所歎。

translations.literal: ") "Happy and easy is the gentleman" - this is what the Major Odes sighs over.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes praise the "happy, easy" prince who becomes father and mother to the people.

sentences.zh: （愷，樂；

translations.literal: (Kai means joy;
translations.idiomatic: (Kai is joy;

sentences.zh: 悌，易也。

translations.literal: ti means easy.
translations.idiomatic: ti is ease.

sentences.zh: 詩大雅泂酌篇曰：「愷悌君子，人之父母。

translations.literal: The "Jiong Zhuo" piece in the Major Odes states: "Happy and easy is the gentleman; he is people's father and mother."
translations.idiomatic: The Major Odes say such a lord is "father and mother of the people."

sentences.zh: 」）布告天下，使明知朕意。

translations.literal: ") Proclaim this to the empire, causing all clearly to know my intent."
translations.idiomatic: Publish this across the realm so all may know my mind."

sentences.zh: 」 酒泉太守段彭討擊車師，大破之。

translations.literal: " Governor Duan Peng of Jiuquan attacked and struck Cheshi and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Peng of Jiuquan attacked Cheshi and won a crushing victory.

sentences.zh: 罷戊己校尉官。

translations.literal: The office of Wu-Ji colonel was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The Wu-Ji colonel post was abolished.

sentences.zh: 二月，武陵澧中蠻叛。

translations.literal: In the second month, the Man of Lizhong in Wuling rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month the Lizhong tribesmen in Wuling rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: （武陵，郡，今澧州。

translations.literal: (Wuling is a commandery, now Lizhou.
translations.idiomatic: (Wuling is modern Hunan west of Dongting.

sentences.zh: 《水經》曰「澧水出武陵充縣西歷山之北」也。

translations.literal: The Water Classic states: "The Li River rises west of Chong county in Wuling, north of Li Mountain."
translations.idiomatic: The Shui jing traces the Li River to the Wuling hills.

sentences.zh: ） 三月甲寅，山陽、東平地震。

translations.literal: ) In the third month, on the day jiayin, Shanyang and Dongping had an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the third month Shanyang and Dongping were shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 己巳，詔曰：「朕以無德，奉承大業，夙夜慄慄，不敢荒寧。

translations.literal: On the day jisi, an edict stated: "I, lacking in virtue, receive the great enterprise; morning and night I tremble in fear; I dare not be idle or at ease.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi an edict said: "Without merit I hold the throne; I tremble night and day and dare not rest.

sentences.zh: （孔安國注《尚書》曰：「不敢荒怠自安寧。

translations.literal: (Kong Anguo's commentary on the Documents states: "I dare not be idle or negligent and seek my own ease."
translations.idiomatic: (Kong Anguo glosses "dare not idle in self-ease."

sentences.zh: 」）而災異仍見，與政相應。

translations.literal: ") Yet calamities and prodigies still appear, corresponding with government.
translations.idiomatic: Yet omens still track my misrule.

sentences.zh: 朕旣不明，涉道日寡；

translations.literal: I am already not enlightened; what I have traversed of the Way grows daily more scant;
translations.idiomatic: I am dim of wit and daily grow less sure of the Way;

sentences.zh: 又選舉乖實，俗吏傷人，官職秏亂，刑罰不中，可不憂與！

translations.literal: moreover recommendations and selections miss the truth, vulgar clerks harm people, offices and posts are wasted and disordered, punishments miss the center - can one not grieve over this!"
translations.idiomatic: recommendations lie, harsh clerks ruin men, offices rot, justice wanders from the mean - how can I not grieve!"

sentences.zh: 昔仲弓季氏之家臣，子游武城之小宰，孔子猶誨以賢才，問以得人。

translations.literal: In olden times Ran Yong was a household minister of the Ji clan, and Zi You was a petty steward of Wucheng; Confucius still instructed them with worthy talent and asked whether they had obtained the right men.
translations.idiomatic: Ran Yong served the Ji family and Zi You tiny Wucheng, yet Confucius still taught them to pick worthy helpers.

sentences.zh: （論語，仲弓爲季氏宰，問政，子曰：「赦小過，舉賢才。

translations.literal: (In the Analects, when Ran Yong was steward of the Ji clan and asked about government, the Master said: "Pardon small faults and raise the worthy and talented."
translations.idiomatic: (The Analects: Confucius told Ran Yong to pardon small faults and promote talent.

sentences.zh: 」子游爲武城宰，孔子謂之曰：「汝得人焉耳乎？

translations.literal: " When Zi You was steward of Wucheng, Confucius said to him: "Have you obtained the right men there?"
translations.idiomatic: He asked Zi You of Wucheng whether he had found good men."

sentences.zh: 」）明政無大小，以得人爲本。

translations.literal: ") Clear government, whether great or small, takes obtaining the right men as its root.
translations.idiomatic: Good government of any scale rests on the right appointments.

sentences.zh: 夫鄉舉里選，必累功勞。

translations.literal: As for village recommendation and neighborhood selection, they must accumulate merit and labor.
translations.idiomatic: Local recommendation must reward proven service.

sentences.zh: 今刺史、守相不明真僞，茂才、孝廉歲以百數，旣非能顯，而當授之政事，甚無謂也。

translations.literal: Now the regional inspectors and shepherds and ministers do not distinguish true from false; flourishing talents and filial and incorrupt candidates come by the hundreds each year; they are already not able to display ability, yet they are to be entrusted with government affairs - this is utterly meaningless.
translations.idiomatic: Today inspectors and governors cannot tell sham from true; hundreds of "flourishing talents" and "filial incorrupts" arrive yearly, most unproved, yet we hand them office - it is absurd.

sentences.zh: 每尋前世舉人貢士，或起甽畒，不繫閥閱。

translations.literal: Whenever I trace how former ages raised men and presented scholars, some rose from the ditches of the fields and were not tied to pedigree.
translations.idiomatic: Past dynasties often raised men from the furrow with no regard for pedigree.

sentences.zh: （《說文》曰：「甽，田中之溝。

translations.literal: (The Shuowen states: "Quan means the ditch in the middle of a field."
translations.idiomatic: (Quan means a field furrow.

sentences.zh: 」音工犬反。

translations.literal: " Its sound is gong-quan fan.
translations.idiomatic: Read gong-quan fan.

sentences.zh: 《史記》曰：「明其等曰閥，積其功曰閱。

translations.literal: The Records states: "To clarify their grade is called fa; to accumulate their merit is called yue."
translations.idiomatic: The Records define noble pedigree as fa and accumulated merit as yue.

sentences.zh: 」言前代舉人務取賢才，不拘門地。

translations.literal: " It means that former ages in raising men strove to take worthy talent and were not constrained by family status.)
translations.idiomatic: Earlier ages chose talent, not gate alone.)

sentences.zh: ）敷奏以言，則文章可採；

translations.literal: When they spread memorials with words, then patterning could be gathered;
translations.idiomatic: When candidates wrote memorials, their skill showed;

sentences.zh: 明試以功，則政有異迹。

translations.literal: when they were clearly tested by merit, then government had unusual traces.
translations.idiomatic: when they were tested in office, their deeds stood out.

sentences.zh: （敷，陳；

translations.literal: (Fu means set forth;
translations.idiomatic: (Fu is "set forth";

sentences.zh: 奏，進也。

translations.literal: zou means advance.
translations.idiomatic: zou is "advance."

sentences.zh: 令各陳進其言，則知其能否也。

translations.literal: It means ordering each to set forth and advance his words, then one knows his ability or inability.
translations.idiomatic: Let each present a memorial to show ability.

sentences.zh: 《尚書》曰「敷奏以言，明試以功」，則政之類。

translations.literal: The Documents states "spread memorials with words, clearly test by merit" - it is of this kind.)
translations.idiomatic: So the Documents pair speech with deed.)

sentences.zh: ）文質彬彬，（彬彬，雜半之貌。

translations.literal: Wen and substance bin-bin - (bin-bin is the appearance of being mixed half and half.
translations.idiomatic: Culture and substance in balance (bin-bin means evenly mixed.

sentences.zh: ）朕甚嘉之。

translations.literal: ) I greatly approve of this.
translations.idiomatic: I greatly approve.

sentences.zh: 其令太傅、三公、中二千石、二千石、郡國守相舉賢良方正、能直言極諫之士各一人。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that the grand tutor, the three dukes, those of attendant two thousand piculs and two thousand piculs, and the shepherds and ministers of commanderies and principalities each recommend one man who is worthy and good, upright in conduct, and able to speak bluntly to the utmost of remonstrance.
translations.idiomatic: Let every grand tutor, duke, two-thousand-picul official, and governor nominate one worthy, upright man willing to speak truth to power.

sentences.zh: 」 夏五月辛酉，初舉孝廉、郎中寬愽有謀，任典城者，以補長、相。

translations.literal: " In summer, in the fifth month, on the day xinyou, for the first time were recommended filial and incorrupt candidates and gentlemen-of-the-palace who were broad, learned, and possessed of strategy, fit to be entrusted with governing a city, in order to fill the posts of chief and minister.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou in the fifth month of summer the court first ordered filial incorrupts and court gentlemen who were learned and shrewd to fill county magistrate and marquis-minister posts.

sentences.zh: （任，堪使也。

translations.literal: (Ren means fit to be commissioned.
translations.idiomatic: (Ren means "fit for duty."

sentences.zh: 典，主也。

translations.literal: Dian means preside.
translations.idiomatic: Dian means "to govern."

sentences.zh: 長謂縣長，相謂侯相。

translations.literal: Chief means county chief; minister means marquis minister.)
translations.idiomatic: Chief is the county magistrate; minister the marquisate chancellor.)

sentences.zh: ） 秋七月辛亥，詔以上林池籞田賦與貧人。

translations.literal: ) In autumn, in the seventh month, on the day xinhai, it was ordered that the Shanglin pond and fenced-field land tax be granted to the poor.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the seventh month of autumn the court gave Shanglin park land to the poor.

sentences.zh: （籞，禁苑也，音語。

translations.literal: (Yu means forbidden park; its sound is yu.
translations.idiomatic: (Yu is the imperial fence; read yu.

sentences.zh: 《前書音義》曰：「折竹以繩懸連之，使人不得往來，謂之籞。

translations.literal: The Former Han Treatise on Sounds and Meanings states: "Bamboo split and linked by rope so that people cannot come and go is called yu."
translations.idiomatic: Han commentaries describe the reed fence around the hunt park.

sentences.zh: 」） 八月庚寅，有星孛于天市。

translations.literal: ") In the eighth month, on the day gengyin, there was a broom star in Celestial Market.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin in the eighth month a comet appeared in the Celestial Market asterism.

sentences.zh: （《史記》曰：「房爲天駟，東北曲十二星曰旗，旗中四星曰天市。

translations.literal: (The Records states: "Fang is the celestial team; the twelve stars curving northeast are called the banner; the four stars in the middle of the banner are called Celestial Market."
translations.idiomatic: (The sky chart places the comet in the market stars near Fang.

sentences.zh: 」） 九月，永昌哀牢夷叛。

translations.literal: ") In the ninth month, the Ailao Yi of Yongchang rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month the Ailao tribes in Yongchang rebelled.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，武陵郡兵討叛蠻，破降之。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, troops of Wuling commandery attacked the rebel Man and defeated and subdued them.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter Wuling forces crushed the rebel tribes.

sentences.zh: 十一月，阜陵王延謀反，貶爲阜陵侯。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, the king of Fuling, Yan, plotted rebellion and was degraded to be marquis of Fuling.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Prince Yan of Fuling lost his kingship for plotting revolt and was reduced to marquis.

sentences.zh: 二年春三月辛丑，詔曰：「比年陰陽不調，飢饉屢臻。

translations.literal: In the second year, in spring, in the third month, on the day xinchou, an edict stated: "In recent years yin and yang have not been in harmony; famine has repeatedly arrived.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 2, xinchou in the third month of spring: yin and yang are out of tune and famine keeps returning.

sentences.zh: 深惟先帝憂人之本，（本謂稼穡。

translations.literal: Deeply I reflect on the late emperor's root in caring for people. (Ben means farming and reaping.
translations.idiomatic: I recall how the late emperor put the farmer first. (Ben means agriculture.

sentences.zh: ）詔《書》曰『不傷財，不害人』，誠欲元元去末歸本。

translations.literal: ) The Documents states "do not harm wealth, do not harm people" - truly he desired the common people to leave the secondary and return to the root.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents forbid wasting wealth or harming the people - he wanted folk to leave commerce for the soil.

sentences.zh: 而今貴戚近親，奢縱無度，嫁聚送終，尤爲僭侈。

translations.literal: Yet now the honored kin and close relatives are extravagant and unrestrained without limit; marriages, gatherings, and sending off the dead are especially presumptuous and excessive.
translations.idiomatic: Yet in-laws and kin still bankrupt themselves on weddings and funerals.

sentences.zh: 有司廢典，莫肯舉察。

translations.literal: The responsible officials neglect the canons; none is willing to investigate and inspect.
translations.idiomatic: Officials ignore the sumptuary laws and will not investigate.

sentences.zh: 春秋之義，以貴理賤。

translations.literal: The meaning of the Spring and Autumn Annals is to use the noble to regulate the base.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn principle is to let the high correct the low.

sentences.zh: 今自三公，並宜明糾非法，宣振威風。

translations.literal: Now from the three dukes downward, all alike should clearly correct what is unlawful and proclaim and shake awesome wind and power.
translations.idiomatic: From the three dukes down every minister must punish excess and show stern authority.

sentences.zh: 朕在弱冠，未知稼穡之艱難，區區管窺，豈能照一隅哉！

translations.literal: I, at the capping of twenty sui, do not know the hardships of sowing and reaping; my petty view through a tube - how can it illumine a single corner!"
translations.idiomatic: I am barely twenty and have not known the plow; my view is a tube peep at the sky - how can I see every corner!"

sentences.zh: （史記扁鵲曰：「以管窺天，以隙視文。

translations.literal: (Bian Que in the Records states: "To view the sky through a tube, to view pattern through a crack."
translations.idiomatic: (Bian Que said a tube cannot span the heavens.

sentences.zh: 」）其科條制度所宜施行，在事者備爲之禁，先京師而後諸夏。

translations.literal: ") As for the statutes and regulations that ought to be put in practice, let those in office fully make prohibitions for them, first in the capital region and afterward in the various Xia.
translations.idiomatic: Draw up the needed bans, begin at the capital, then extend to the provinces.

sentences.zh: 」（《公羊傳》曰：「春秋內中國而外諸夏，內諸侯而外夷狄。

translations.literal: " (The Gongyang Commentary states: "In the Spring and Autumn Annals the inner is the central states and the outer is the various Xia; the inner is the feudatories and the outer is the Yi and Di.
translations.idiomatic: (The Gongyang says the classic ranks inner China and outer barbarians.

sentences.zh: 王者欲一乎天下，曷以內外之辭言？

translations.literal: The king desires to unify the empire - why speak in terms of inner and outer?"
translations.idiomatic: The king would unify all under Heaven - why inner and outer?"
