sentences.zh: 詔曰：「朕新離供養，（去年馬太后崩。

translations.literal: An edict stated: "I have newly parted from support and nurture. (Last year Empress Dowager Ma died.)
translations.idiomatic: An edict said: "I have just lost my mother's care. (The empress dowager died last year.

sentences.zh: ）愆咎衆著，上天降異，大變隨之。

translations.literal: ) Faults and blame are many and manifest; High Heaven sends down prodigies; great changes follow upon them.
translations.idiomatic: My faults show plain; Heaven sends portents and great changes follow.

sentences.zh: 《詩》不云乎：『亦孔之醜。

translations.literal: Does not the 《Odes》 state: 'It is also very ugly.
translations.idiomatic: The 《Odes》 say, "This is a hideous thing.

sentences.zh: 』（詩小雅曰：「朔月辛卯，日有食之，亦孔之醜。

translations.literal: ' (The "Minor Odes" states: "On the new moon, xinmao, there was an eclipse of the sun—it is also very ugly."
translations.idiomatic: ' (The ode on the xinmao eclipse calls it "hideous.

sentences.zh: 」孔，甚也。

translations.literal: " Kong means very.
translations.idiomatic: Kong means "very."

sentences.zh: 醜，惡也。

translations.literal: Chou means evil.
translations.idiomatic: Chou means "shameful."

sentences.zh: ）又乆旱傷麥，憂心慘切。

translations.literal: ) Moreover for long drought has injured wheat; anxious care is bitter and cutting.
translations.idiomatic: ) Long drought has ruined the wheat, and grief cuts deep.

sentences.zh: 公卿已下，其舉直言極諫、能指朕過失者各一人，遣詣公車，將親覽問焉。

translations.literal: From the dukes and ministers downward, let each recommend one man who speaks bluntly to the utmost of remonstrance and is able to point out my faults and errors; dispatch them to the public carriage gate; I will personally peruse and question them.
translations.idiomatic: Let every minister nominate one blunt critic of my faults and send him to the public carriage office; I will read his words myself.

sentences.zh: 其以巖穴爲先，（《前書》鄒陽曰：「顯巖穴之士。

translations.literal: Let those in mountain caves be given priority. (Zou Yang in the Former Han stated: "Make manifest the gentlemen of mountain caves."
translations.idiomatic: Favor recluses over courtiers. (Zou Yang asked to elevate cave scholars.

sentences.zh: 」）勿取浮華。

translations.literal: ") Do not take flashy emptiness."
translations.idiomatic: Do not pick empty show-offs."

sentences.zh: 」 甲申，詔曰：「春秋書『無麥苗』，重之也。

translations.literal: " On the day jiashen, an edict stated: "The Spring and Autumn Annals records 'no wheat or young plants'—it treats this as weighty.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen an edict said: "The classic records 'no wheat, no shoots' as a grave matter.

sentences.zh: （春秋莊公七年：「秋，大水，無麥苗。

translations.literal: (Duke Zhuang, seventh year of the Spring and Autumn Annals: "In autumn, great flood; no wheat or young plants."
translations.idiomatic: (Zhuang 7: autumn flood destroyed wheat and shoots.

sentences.zh: 」《公羊傳》曰：「一災不書，待無麥然後書無苗。

translations.literal: " The Gongyang Commentary states: "When one disaster occurs it is not written; it waits until there is no wheat and then writes no young plants."
translations.idiomatic: The Gongyang says one calamity is not recorded alone; only when both fail does the text say so.

sentences.zh: 」何休注曰：「不書穀，至麥苗獨書，人食最重也。

translations.literal: " He Xiu's commentary states: "It does not write grain; only when it reaches wheat and young plants alone does it write—human food is the weightiest."
translations.idiomatic: He Xiu notes the classic stresses human food by naming wheat and shoots.

sentences.zh: 」）去秋雨澤不適，今時復旱，如炎如焚。

translations.literal: ") Last autumn's rains and moisture were not timely; now the season again is drought, like flame, like burning.
translations.idiomatic: Last autumn's rains failed; now drought returns like scorching fire.

sentences.zh: （炎、焚言熱氣甚。

translations.literal: (Yan and fen speak of heat qi being extreme.
translations.idiomatic: (Yan and fen picture fierce heat.

sentences.zh: 韓詩：「旱魃爲虐，如炎如焚。

translations.literal: Han Odes: "The drought demon works havoc, like flame, like burning."
translations.idiomatic: The Han version of the Odes says the drought fiend "burns like fire."

sentences.zh: 」）凶年無時，而爲備未至。

translations.literal: ") In a bad year there is no timely moment, yet preparations have not arrived.
translations.idiomatic: Famine comes without warning, yet we are not ready.

sentences.zh: 朕之不德，上累三光，震慄忉忉，痛心疾首。

translations.literal: My lack of virtue above drags down the three luminaries; I tremble in anxious fear; my heart aches as if sick unto death.
translations.idiomatic: My faults dim sun, moon, and stars; I tremble and my head aches with shame.

sentences.zh: （忉音刀。

translations.literal: (Dao is read with the sound dao.
translations.idiomatic: (Read dao for the graph glossed as anxious grief.

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：「憂心忉忉。

translations.literal: The 《Odes》 states: "Anxious care is dao-dao."
translations.idiomatic: The 《Odes》 speak of "dao-dao" care.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「疢如疾首。

translations.literal: " It also states: "Like sickness in the head."
translations.idiomatic: They also compare grief to sickness in the head.

sentences.zh: 」）前代聖君，博思咨諏，（咨諏，謀也，音子余反。

translations.literal: ") Former-age sage rulers broadly pondered and consulted. (Zi-yu means planning; its sound is zi-yu fan.)
translations.idiomatic: Ancient sages sought wide counsel. (Zi-yu means "to plan," read zi-yu.

sentences.zh: ）雖降災咎，輒有開匱反風之應。

translations.literal: ) Although calamities and blame descended, thereupon there were responses of opening the coffer and winds reversing.
translations.idiomatic: When disaster struck, they opened the golden coffer and winds turned fair.

sentences.zh: （武王有疾，周公作請命之書，藏於金匱。

translations.literal: (When King Wu was ill, the Duke of Zhou composed the prayer for Mandate and stored it in the metal coffer.
translations.idiomatic: (King Wu fell ill; the Duke of Zhou hid a prayer in the golden coffer.

sentences.zh: 後管、蔡流言，成王疑周公，天乃大風，禾木盡偃。

translations.literal: Afterward Guan and Cai spread slander; King Cheng suspected the Duke of Zhou; Heaven thereupon sent a great wind and grain and trees all blew down flat.
translations.idiomatic: When Guan and Cai slandered him, King Cheng doubted the duke; a gale flattened the fields.

sentences.zh: 成王啟金匱，得書，乃郊天謝過，天乃反風起禾。

translations.literal: King Cheng opened the metal coffer, obtained the document, thereupon sacrificed at the suburb to Heaven apologizing for his fault; Heaven thereupon sent contrary winds and the grain rose.
translations.idiomatic: Cheng opened the coffer, read the prayer, sacrificed to Heaven, and the storm reversed.

sentences.zh: 事見尚書。

translations.literal: The affair appears in the Documents.)
translations.idiomatic: The story is in the 《Documents》.

sentences.zh: ）令予小子，徒慘慘而已。

translations.literal: ) Now I, the tiny child, only suffer bitterly, that is all.
translations.idiomatic: I, least of men, can only grieve.

sentences.zh: 其令二千石理冤獄，錄輕繫；

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that those of two thousand piculs set right wrongful imprisonment and record and release those lightly bound;
translations.idiomatic: Let two-thousand-picul officials clear unjust jails and free minor prisoners;

sentences.zh: 禱五嶽四瀆，及名山能興雲致雨者，兾蒙不崇朝徧雨天下之報。

translations.literal: pray to the five marchmounts and four great rivers, and to famous mountains able to raise clouds and bring down rain, hoping to receive the response of rain covering the empire in less than a full morning.
translations.idiomatic: pray at the five peaks and four rivers and at rain-making mountains, hoping for a morning shower over the whole realm.

sentences.zh: （尚書大傳曰：「五嶽皆觸石出雲，膚寸而合，不崇朝而雨天下。

translations.literal: (The Great Commentary on the Documents states: "The five marchmounts all strike stone and send out clouds; a skin-inch combines, and in less than a full morning they rain on the empire."
translations.idiomatic: (The Documents commentary says the sacred peaks can cloud over and rain the realm before noon.

sentences.zh: 」）務加肅敬焉。

translations.literal: ") By all means increase solemn reverence."
translations.idiomatic: Perform these rites with utmost reverence."

sentences.zh: 」 三月甲寅，詔曰：「孔子曰：『刑罰不中，則人無所措手足。

translations.literal: " In the third month, on the day jiayin, an edict stated: "Confucius stated: 'If punishments miss the center, then the people have nowhere to put hand or foot.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the third month an edict quoted Confucius: "When punishments miss the mark, the people are lost.

sentences.zh: 』今吏多不良，擅行喜怒，或案不以罪，迫脅無辜，致令自殺者，一歲且多於斷獄，甚非爲人父母之意也。

translations.literal: ' Now the clerks are mostly not good; they act on whim of joy and anger; some examine cases not according to crime, coerce the guiltless, causing them to commit suicide—in one year this is about to become more frequent than cases decided in court—this is utterly not the intent of those who are father and mother to the people.
translations.idiomatic: Too many clerks are cruel: they bully the innocent into suicide more often than courts convict—this is not how fathers and mothers of the people should rule.

sentences.zh: （《書》曰：「元后作人父母。

translations.literal: (The Documents states: "The primal king acts as father and mother to the people."
translations.idiomatic: (The 《Documents》 call the king father and mother to the people.

sentences.zh: 」）有司其議糾舉之。

translations.literal: ") Let the responsible officials deliberate on investigating and impeaching this."
translations.idiomatic: Let ministers draft measures to stop it."

sentences.zh: 」 荊、豫諸郡兵討破武陵漊中叛蠻。

translations.literal: " Troops of the various commanderies of Jing and Yu attacked and defeated the rebel Man of Louzhong in Wuling.
translations.idiomatic: Forces from Jing and Yu crushed the Louzhong rebels in Wuling.

sentences.zh: 夏五月辛亥，詔曰：「朕思遟直士，側席異聞。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fifth month, on the day xinhai, an edict stated: "I ponder delayed encounter with upright gentlemen; I shift my mat and seek unusual hearing.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the fifth month of summer an edict said: "I long for honest men I have yet to meet; I set aside the mat and listen for what I have not heard.

sentences.zh: （遲猶希望也，音持二反。

translations.literal: (Chi means hope; its sound is chi-er fan.)
translations.idiomatic: Chi here means "to long for," read chi-er fan.

sentences.zh: 側席謂不正坐，所以待賢良也。

translations.literal: "Shifting the mat sideways" means not sitting squarely; thereby one awaits the worthy and good.
translations.idiomatic: A tilted mat meant the ruler left a seat empty for a worthy man.

sentences.zh: ）其先至者，各以發憤吐懣，略聞子大夫之志矣，皆欲置於左右，顧問省納。

translations.literal: ) As for those who came first, each with roused resentment pouring out grievance—I have briefly heard the intent of you, gentlemen; I all desire to place you at my left and right for consultation and to examine and accept.
translations.idiomatic: Those who came first spoke with passion; I have heard something of your minds and mean to keep you near for counsel.

sentences.zh: 建武詔書又曰，堯試臣以職，不直以言語筆札。

translations.literal: The Jianwu edict also stated: Yao tested ministers by office, not merely by spoken words and brush slips.
translations.idiomatic: A Jianwu edict recalled that Yao tried officials in post, not on paper alone.

sentences.zh: （書舜典曰：「朕其試哉。

translations.literal: (The Canon of Shun in the Documents states: "I will try them!"
translations.idiomatic: (The Canon of Shun says, "I will try them."

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「歷試諸難。

translations.literal: " It also states: "Through trials in many hardships."
translations.idiomatic: It also speaks of testing through hardship.

sentences.zh: 」札，簡也。

translations.literal: " Zha means bamboo slip.)
translations.idiomatic: Zha means a writing slip.)

sentences.zh: ）今外官多曠，並可以補任。

translations.literal: ) Now many outside offices are vacant; they may all be filled by appointment.
translations.idiomatic: Many provincial posts stand empty and may now be filled.

sentences.zh: 」 戊辰，太傅趙憙薨。

translations.literal: " On the day wuchen, the grand tutor Zhao Xi died.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen Grand Tutor Zhao Xi died.

sentences.zh: 冬，始行月令迎氣樂。

translations.literal: In winter, for the first time was performed the music of the Monthly Ordinances welcoming the seasonal qi.
translations.idiomatic: That winter the court first used the Monthly Ordinances music to greet each season.

sentences.zh: （《東觀記》曰：「馬防上言，『聖人作樂，所以宣氣致和，順陰陽也。

translations.literal: (The Eastern Lodge Record states: "Ma Fang submitted a memorial saying, 'The sages made music in order to spread qi and bring harmony, complying with yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: (The Dongguan ji records Ma Fang urging seasonal music to harmonize yin and yang.

sentences.zh: 臣愚以爲可因歲首發太蔟之律，奏雅頌之音，以迎和氣。

translations.literal: Your servant foolishly thinks one may at the year's beginning issue the pitch of great cluster, perform the elegant and eulogy tones, in order to welcome harmonizing qi.
translations.idiomatic: He proposed opening the year with the great-cluster pitch and classical hymns.

sentences.zh: 』時以作樂器費多，遂獨行十月迎氣樂也。

translations.literal: ' At the time because making instruments cost much, thereupon they alone performed the tenth-month qi-welcoming music."
translations.idiomatic: Cost killed the full plan; only the tenth-month qi rite was kept.

sentences.zh: 」） 是歲，零陵獻芝草。

translations.literal: ") That year, Lingling presented lingzhi.
translations.idiomatic: That year Lingling sent lingzhi as tribute.

sentences.zh: 有八黃龍見於泉陵。

translations.literal: There were eight yellow dragons seen at Quanling.
translations.idiomatic: Eight yellow dragons were sighted at Quanling.

sentences.zh: （《伏侯古今注》曰：「見零陵泉陵湘水中，相與戲。

translations.literal: (The Fu Hou Notes Ancient and Modern states: "They appeared in the Xiang River at Quanling, Lingling, sporting together.
translations.idiomatic: (They sported in the Xiang River at Quanling.

sentences.zh: 其二大如馬，有角；

translations.literal: Two of them were as large as horses and had horns;
translations.idiomatic: Two were horse-sized and horned;

sentences.zh: 六枚大如駒，無角。

translations.literal: six were as large as colts and had no horns."
translations.idiomatic: six were colt-sized and hornless.

sentences.zh: 」）西域假司馬班超擊疏勒，破之。

translations.literal: ") Acting chief commandant of the Western Regions Ban Chao attacked Su-le and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao then broke the kingdom of Su-le.

sentences.zh: 六年春二月辛卯，琅邪王京薨。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, in spring, in the second month, on the day xinmao, the king of Langye, Jing, died.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 6, xinmao in the second month of spring: King Jing of Langye died.

sentences.zh: 夏五月辛酉，趙王盱薨。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fifth month, on the day xinyou, the king of Zhao, Xu, died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou in the fifth month of summer King Xu of Zhao died.

sentences.zh: 六月丙辰，太尉鮑昱薨。

translations.literal: On the day bingchen in the sixth month, the grand commandant Bao Yu died.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen in the sixth month Grand Commandant Bao Yu died.

sentences.zh: 辛未晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On the last day of the month xinwei, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: At month's end on xinwei the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 秋七月癸巳，以大司農鄧彪爲太尉。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the seventh month, on the day guisi, Deng Biao, grand minister of agriculture, was made grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On guisi in the seventh month Grand Minister of Agriculture Deng Biao became grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 七年春正月，沛王輔、濟南王康、東平王蒼、中山王焉、東海王政、琅邪王宇來朝。

translations.literal: In the seventh year, in spring, in the first month, the kings Fu of Pei, Kang of Jinan, Cang of Dongping, Yan of Zhongshan, Zheng of Donghai, and Yu of Langye came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 7, first month of spring: Princes Fu, Kang, Cang, Yan, Zheng, and Yu attended court.

sentences.zh: 夏六月甲寅，廢皇太子慶爲清河王，立皇子肈爲皇太子。

translations.literal: In summer, in the sixth month, on the day jiayin, the heir apparent Qing was deposed and made king of Qinghe; the imperial son Zhao was established as heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the sixth month Crown Prince Qing was demoted to Prince of Qinghe and Prince Zhao was named heir in his place.

sentences.zh: 己未，徙廣平王羨爲西平王。

translations.literal: On the day jiwei, the king of Guangping, Xian, was moved to be king of Xiping.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei Prince Xian of Guangping became Prince of Xiping.

sentences.zh: 秋八月，飲酎高廟，禘祭光武皇帝、孝明皇帝。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the eighth month, strained ale was drunk at the lofty temple; the di sacrifice was performed to Emperor Guangwu and Emperor Xiaoming.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month of autumn the court tasted the autumn ale at Gaozu's temple and offered the grand di to Guangwu and Ming.

sentences.zh: （前書高廟飲酎，奏武德、五行之舞。

translations.literal: (The Former Han states that at the lofty temple strained ale was drunk and the dances of Wu De and Wu Xing were performed.
translations.idiomatic: (Former Han had used the Wu De and Wu Xing dances at that rite.

sentences.zh: 音義云：「正月旦作酒，八月成，名曰酎者，言醇也。

translations.literal: Sounds and meanings explain: "Wine begun on the first day of the first month, completed in the eighth month, is called zhou—meaning rich."
translations.idiomatic: Commentaries explain zhou ale, brewed New Year's day and ready by autumn, as especially strong.

sentences.zh: 」武帝時因八月甞酎，令諸侯出金助祭，所謂酎金也。

translations.literal: " Under Emperor Wu, because in the eighth month the new zhou was tasted, it was ordered that the marquels contribute gold to assist sacrifice—this is what is called zhou gold.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu made marquels pay gold toward the zhou offering.

sentences.zh: 丁孚漢儀式曰：「九真、交阯、日南者用犀角二，長九寸，若瑇瑁甲一；

translations.literal: Ding Fu's Han Ritual Models states: "For Jiuzhen, Jiaozhi, and Rinan use two rhinoceros horns nine inches long, or one tortoise shell;
translations.idiomatic: Southern commanderies could substitute rhino horn or tortoise shell for gold.

sentences.zh: 鬱林用象牙一，長三尺已上，若翠羽各二十，準以當金。

translations.literal: for Yulin use one tusk three chi or more in length, or twenty kingfisher feathers each, reckoned as equivalent to gold."
translations.idiomatic: Yulin could send ivory or kingfisher plumes in lieu of gold.

sentences.zh: 」）甲辰，詔：「《書》云『祖考來假』，明哲之祀。

translations.literal: ") On the day jiachen, an edict: "The Documents states 'Ancestors come to visit'—this is the sacrifice of the wise and enlightened.
translations.idiomatic: On jiachen an edict quoted the Documents: "The ancestors draw near" at a wise ruler's sacrifice.

sentences.zh: （假音格。

translations.literal: (Jia is read with the sound ge.
translations.idiomatic: (Read ge for the word glossed as "visit."

sentences.zh: 格，至也。

translations.literal: Ge means arrive.
translations.idiomatic: It means "to arrive."

sentences.zh: 尚書夔曰：「於！

translations.literal: Kui in the Documents states: "Ah!
translations.idiomatic: Kui in the Documents cries, "Ah!

sentences.zh: 予擊石拊石，搏拊琴瑟以詠，祖考來格。

translations.literal: I strike the stones, tap the stones, beat and strum zithers and lutes to chant; the ancestors come to visit."
translations.idiomatic: I strike stone and strum strings until the ancestors draw near."

sentences.zh: 」言明哲祭祀，則能致祖考之神來至。

translations.literal: " It means that when the wise and enlightened sacrifice, they can bring the spirits of ancestors and fathers to come.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss says true sacrifice draws the dead to the hall.

sentences.zh: ）予末小子，質又菲薄，仰惟先帝烝烝之情，前修禘祭，以盡孝敬。

translations.literal: ) I, the least child, am moreover coarse and thin; looking up I reflect on the late emperor's warm thick affection; formerly I performed the di sacrifice to exhaust filial respect.
translations.idiomatic: I am young and slight of merit; thinking of my father's love I have already held a di to show filial piety.

sentences.zh: 朕得識昭穆之序，寄遠祖之思。

translations.literal: I have been able to know the order of zhao and mu and to lodge longing for a remote ancestor.
translations.idiomatic: Thus I learned the zhao-mu order and honored a distant forebear.

sentences.zh: 今年大禮復舉，加以先帝之坐，（言顯宗神坐，今新加之。

translations.literal: This year the great rites are again held, with the addition of the late emperor's seat. (It means Emperor Xianzong's spirit seat, now newly added.
translations.idiomatic: This year's grand rite adds Emperor Ming's spirit tablet.

sentences.zh: ）悲傷感懷。

translations.literal: ) Grief and sorrow touch the heart.
translations.idiomatic: Grief fills me.

sentences.zh: 樂以迎來，哀以送徃，雖祭亡如在，而空虛不知所裁，庶或饗之。

translations.literal: Music welcomes what comes; grief sends off what goes; although in sacrifice the gone are as if present, yet empty and void I do not know how to regulate it—perhaps they will enjoy it.
translations.idiomatic: Music greets the spirits, mourning sees them off; though the classic says the dead are "as if present," I feel hollow and hardly know how to proceed—yet I hope they feast.

sentences.zh: 豈亡克慎肅雍之臣，辟公之相，（肅，敬；

translations.literal: Is it that there are none who can be scrupulous, reverent, harmonious, and yielding ministers, assistants to the regional lords? (Su means reverent;
translations.idiomatic: Surely I have ministers reverent and mild to assist the rites? (Su is reverence;

sentences.zh: 雍，和；

translations.literal: yong means harmonious;
translations.idiomatic: yong is harmony;

sentences.zh: 相，助也。

translations.literal: xiang means help.
translations.idiomatic: xiang means aid.

sentences.zh: 詩大雅曰：「有來雍雍，至止肅肅，相維辟公，天子穆穆。

translations.literal: The Major Odes states: "They came in harmonious harmony; they arrived in solemn solemnity; they assisted, these regional lords; the Son of Heaven was grave and dignified."
translations.idiomatic: The Major Odes describe feudatories assisting at sacrifice in solemn harmony.

sentences.zh: 」言百辟諸侯來助祭，皆有肅雍之德，無懈慢也。

translations.literal: " It means that the hundred regional lords who came to assist at sacrifice all had reverent and harmonious virtue, without slackness.
translations.idiomatic: Every prince who shared the rite showed grave respect.

sentences.zh: ）皆助朕之依依。

translations.literal: ") All assist my lingering reluctance.
translations.idiomatic: You all steady me in this long grief.

sentences.zh: （依依，思慕之意。

translations.literal: (Yi-yi means the idea of longing and admiring.)
translations.idiomatic: (Yi-yi is tender longing.)

sentences.zh: ）今賜公錢四十萬，卿半之，及百官執事各有差。

translations.literal: ) Now let there be granted to the dukes four hundred thousand cash, to the ministers half that, and to the hundred officials holding office each according to a graded scale."
translations.idiomatic: Grant the dukes four hundred thousand cash, ministers half, and other officials graded sums."

sentences.zh: 」 九月甲戌，幸偃師，東涉卷津，（卷，縣名，屬河南郡也。

translations.literal: " In the ninth month, on the day jiaxu, he favored Yanshi and crossed east at Juan Ford. (Juan is a county name belonging to Henan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: On jiaxu in the ninth month he went to Yanshi and crossed east at Juan. (Juan county lay in Henan.

sentences.zh: 卷音丘權反。

translations.literal: Juan is read with the sound qiu-quan fan.)
translations.idiomatic: Read juan with the qiu initial and quan rime.)

sentences.zh: ）至河內。

translations.literal: ) He arrived in Henei.
translations.idiomatic: He entered Henei.

sentences.zh: 下詔曰：「車駕行秋稼，觀收穫，因涉郡界。

translations.literal: He issued an edict stating: "The imperial carriage travels to view the autumn grain and observe the harvest; thereby it crosses commandery boundaries.
translations.idiomatic: An edict said: "This tour is to see the autumn harvest as we pass through the provinces.

sentences.zh: 皆精騎輕行，無它輜重。

translations.literal: All use picked cavalry and light travel; there are no other baggage trains.
translations.idiomatic: We ride light with no baggage train.

sentences.zh: 不得輒修道橋，遠離城郭，遣吏逢迎，刺探起居，（刺探謂候伺也。

translations.literal: Do not on your own repair roads and bridges, keep far from walls and moats, dispatch clerks to meet and escort, or spy out comings and goings. (Ci-tan means to wait and watch.
translations.idiomatic: Do not rebuild roads, meet the train far out, or spy on my movements. (Ci-tan means to scout.

sentences.zh: 探音湯勘反。

translations.literal: Tan is read with the sound tang-kan fan.)
translations.idiomatic: Read tan tang-kan fan.)

sentences.zh: ）出入前後，以爲煩擾。

translations.literal: ) Going out and coming in before and after—take this as vexation.
translations.idiomatic: Such bustle only wearies the people.

sentences.zh: 動務省約，但患不能脫粟瓢飲耳。

translations.literal: Actions must be economical; I only worry that I cannot eat hulled millet and drink from a gourd.
translations.idiomatic: Keep things spare; I would be content with plain millet and gourd water.

sentences.zh: （晏子相齊，食脫粟之飯。

translations.literal: (Yan Zi as minister of Qi ate hulled millet rice.
translations.idiomatic: (Yan Ying ate coarse grain in Qi.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰，顏回一瓢飲。

translations.literal: Confucius stated that Yan Hui drank from one gourd.)
translations.idiomatic: Confucius praised Yan Hui for living on a single gourd of drink.)

sentences.zh: ）所過欲令貧弱有利，無違詔書。

translations.literal: ) Wherever we pass, I desire that the poor and weak have benefit; do not disobey the edict."
translations.idiomatic: Let every stop help the poor and break no part of this order."

sentences.zh: 」遂覽淇園。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he inspected the Qi Garden.
translations.idiomatic: He then toured the Qi Garden.

sentences.zh: （《前書音義》曰：「淇園，衞之苑也。

translations.literal: (The Former Han Treatise on Sounds and Meanings states: "The Qi Garden was the park of Wei."
translations.idiomatic: (The Qi Garden was the old Wei royal park.

sentences.zh: 」）己酉，進幸鄴，勞饗魏郡守令已下，至于三老、門闌、走卒，賜錢各有差。

translations.literal: ") On the day jiyou, he advanced in favor to Ye, consoled with gifts and feasted the grand administrator of Wei and the magistrates and chiefs downward to the three elders, gate guards, and runners, granting cash each according to a graded scale.
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou he reached Ye, feasted Wei officials from governor down to gate guards, and gave cash by rank.

sentences.zh: 勞賜常山、趙國吏人，復元氏租賦三歲。

translations.literal: He consoled with gifts the clerks and people of Changshan and Zhao commanderies and remitted three years of rent and tax for Yuanshi.
translations.idiomatic: He rewarded Changshan and Zhao staff and remitted three years of taxes in Yuanshi.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On the day xinmao, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao the court returned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 詔天下繫囚減死一等，勿笞，詣邊戍；

translations.literal: It was ordered that prisoners in the empire have death sentences reduced one grade, not be flogged, and go to frontier garrison;
translations.idiomatic: Death sentences empire-wide were commuted one degree, the rod waived, and convicts sent to the frontier;

sentences.zh: 妻子自隨，占著所在；

translations.literal: wives and children might follow of their own accord and register residence where they were;
translations.idiomatic: wives and children could follow and register there;

sentences.zh: 父母同產欲相從者，恣聽之；

translations.literal: where parents or full siblings wished to follow along, let them be heard as they will;
translations.idiomatic: parents or full brothers could join them if they wished;

sentences.zh: 有不到者，皆以乏軍興論。

translations.literal: those who did not arrive were all to be judged by the statute on lacking for military exigency.
translations.idiomatic: no-shows were charged as if they had blocked military supply.

sentences.zh: （軍興而致闕乏，當死刑也。

translations.literal: (When military exigency is raised and there is lack, it is liable to capital sentence.)
translations.idiomatic: (That statute carried a capital penalty.)

sentences.zh: ）及犯殊死，一切募下蠶室；

translations.literal: ) As for those guilty of execution by dismemberment, in every case recruit them down to the silkworm chamber;
translations.idiomatic: Those facing the worst death were castrated instead;

sentences.zh: 其女子宮。

translations.literal: the women went to palace labor.
translations.idiomatic: women convicts went to palace labor.

sentences.zh: 繫囚鬼薪、白粲已上，（《前書》曰：「鬼薪、白粲已上，皆三歲刑也。

translations.literal: Prisoners at hard labor for gui-xin and white-rice and above. (The Former Han states: "Gui-xin and white-rice upward are all punishments of three years.
translations.idiomatic: Prisoners sentenced to gui-xin, white-rice, or worse. (Former Han defined those as three-year labor.

sentences.zh: 男子爲鬼薪，取薪以給宗廟。

translations.literal: Men as gui-xin gather firewood to supply the ancestral temples.
translations.idiomatic: Men on gui-xin gathered temple fuel.

sentences.zh: 女子爲白粲，使擇米白粲粲然。

translations.literal: Women as white-rice are made to select rice until it is white and gleaming."
translations.idiomatic: Women on white-rice sifted grain until it shone white.

sentences.zh: 」）皆減本罪各一等，輸司寇作。

translations.literal: ") In every case their original crimes were reduced one grade and they were sent to minister-of-crimes labor.
translations.idiomatic: All such sentences were cut one degree to minister-of-crimes corvee.

sentences.zh: 亡命贖：死罪入縑二十匹，右趾至髡鉗城旦舂十匹，完城旦至司寇三匹，吏人有罪未發覺，詔書到自告者，半入贖。

translations.literal: Fugitives from the law might ransom: for death, twenty bolts of fine silk; for right-toe amputation down through shaved-head wall-dawn labor, ten bolts; for intact wall-dawn labor down to minister-of-crimes labor, three bolts; for clerks and people whose crimes had not yet been discovered, those who confessed themselves when the edict arrived paid half the ransom.
translations.idiomatic: Fugitives could buy off sentences on a fixed scale; self-surrender when the edict arrived halved the price.

sentences.zh: 冬十月癸丑，西巡狩，幸長安。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, on the day guichou, he toured west on a hunt and favored Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou in the tenth month of winter he began a western progress to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，祠高廟，遂有事十一陵。

translations.literal: On the day bingchen, he sacrificed at the lofty temple, then performed the full rites at the eleven mausolea.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen he worshipped at Gaozu's temple and the eleven Western Han tombs.

sentences.zh: 遣使者祠太上皇於萬年，（太上皇，高祖父也，名煓，音它官反，一名執嘉。

translations.literal: He sent envoys to sacrifice with the middle victim to the Supreme Progenitor at Wannian. (The Supreme Progenitor was Gaozu's father, named Tuan, read ta-guan fan, also called Zhijia.
translations.idiomatic: Envoys sacrificed at Wannian to Liu Taigong, Gaozu's father (also named Zhijia.

sentences.zh: 《三輔黃圖》曰，高祖初都櫟陽，太上皇崩，葬櫟陽北原陵，號萬年，仍分置萬年縣，在今櫟陽東北，故就祭祀焉。

translations.literal: The Three Adjuncts Yellow Chart states: When Gaozu first made Duyang his capital, the Supreme Progenitor died and was buried on the plain north of Duyang, called Wannian; thereupon Wannian county was separately established; it is now northeast of Duyang—therefore sacrifice was performed there.)
translations.idiomatic: His tomb north of Duyang was called Wannian; a county of that name was split off for sacrifice there.)

sentences.zh: ）以中牢祠蕭何、霍光。

translations.literal: With the middle victim he sacrificed to Xiao He and Huo Guang.
translations.idiomatic: He offered ox and sheep at the shrines of Xiao He and Huo Guang.

sentences.zh: 進幸槐里。

translations.literal: He advanced in favor to Huaili.
translations.idiomatic: He continued to Huaili.

sentences.zh: 岐山得銅器，形似酒罇，獻之。

translations.literal: At Mount Qi a bronze vessel was obtained, shaped like a wine jar, and was presented.
translations.idiomatic: A bronze jar-shaped vessel found on Mount Qi was sent to court.

sentences.zh: 又獲白鹿。

translations.literal: Moreover a white deer was captured.
translations.idiomatic: A white deer was taken as well.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：「上無明天子，下無賢方伯。

translations.literal: The emperor stated: "Above there is no enlightened Son of Heaven; below there is no worthy regional lord.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "With no enlightened sovereign above and no worthy shepherds below,

sentences.zh: （已見明帝紀。

translations.literal: (Already seen in the annals of Emperor Ming.)
translations.idiomatic: (As noted under Emperor Ming.

sentences.zh: ）『人之無良，相怨一方。

translations.literal: 'When men lack goodness, they blame one another in their quarter.'
translations.idiomatic: The Odes say, "When men lack goodness they blame one another."

sentences.zh: 』（詩小雅也。

translations.literal: ' (This is from the "Minor Odes."
translations.idiomatic: (Minor Odes.

sentences.zh: 良，善也。

translations.literal: Liang means good.
translations.idiomatic: Liang means "good."

sentences.zh: 言王者所爲無有善者，各相與於一方而怨之。

translations.literal: It means that what the king does has nothing good in it; they blame one another each in their quarter.
translations.idiomatic: It blames a ruler whose deeds are not good.

sentences.zh: 義見韓詩。

translations.literal: The meaning appears in the Han Odes.)
translations.idiomatic: The Han version of the Odes gives this gloss.)

sentences.zh: ）斯器亦曷爲來哉？

translations.literal: ) For what purpose has this vessel also come?"
translations.idiomatic: Why, then, has this bronze come to me?"

sentences.zh: 」（《公羊傳》曰：「孔子抱麟而泣曰：『孰爲來哉？

translations.literal: " (The Gongyang Commentary states: "Confucius embraced the unicorn and wept, saying, 'Who has brought you? Who has brought you?
translations.idiomatic: He echoed Confucius at the unicorn: "Who sent you? Who sent you?

sentences.zh: 孰爲來哉？

translations.literal: Who has brought you?"
translations.idiomatic: Who sent you?"

sentences.zh: 』」）又幸長平，御池陽宮，（《前書音義》曰：「長平阪在池陽南，有長平觀，去長安五十餘里。

translations.literal: '") Moreover he favored Changping and visited the Chiyang Palace. (The Former Han Treatise on Sounds and Meanings states: "The Changping slope is south of Chiyang; there is a Changping lookout; it is more than fifty li from Chang'an."
translations.idiomatic: He also stopped at Changping and the Chiyang palace. (Changping ridge lay south of Chiyang, some fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 」）東至高陵，造舟於涇而還。

translations.literal: ") Eastward to Gaoling, he built boats on the Jing and returned.
translations.idiomatic: He went east to Gaoling, linked boats on the Jing to ford, and turned back.

sentences.zh: （造，至也。

translations.literal: (Zao means arrive.)
translations.idiomatic: (Zao here means "to reach."

sentences.zh: 謂次比舟，令相至爲橋而度也。

translations.literal: It means ranking boats in sequence, making them reach one another to form a bridge and cross.)
translations.idiomatic: Boats were chained into a floating bridge.

sentences.zh: 《爾雅》曰：「天子造舟，諸侯維舟，大夫方舟，士特舟。

translations.literal: The Erya states: "The Son of Heaven zao zhou, feudatories wei zhou, grandees fang zhou, gentlemen te zhou."
translations.idiomatic: The Erya ranks royal and noble boat bridges by rank.

sentences.zh: 」）每所到幸，輒會郡縣吏人，勞賜作樂。

translations.literal: ) Whenever he favored a place in passing, he always assembled the commandery and county clerks and people, consoled them with gifts, and had music performed.
translations.idiomatic: At each stop he called in local officials, feasted them, and had music played.

sentences.zh: 十一月，詔勞賜河東守、令、掾以下。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, it was ordered to console with gifts the governor of Hedong, magistrates, and assistants downward.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month he rewarded Hedong's governor, magistrates, and staff down to assistants.

sentences.zh: 十二月丁亥，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On the day dinghai in the twelfth month, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai in the twelfth month the court returned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 是歲，京師及郡國螟。

translations.literal: That year, the capital region and the commanderies and principalities had crop worms.
translations.idiomatic: That year locusts or caterpillars struck the capital and provinces.

sentences.zh: 八年春正月壬辰，東平王蒼薨。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, in spring, in the first month, on the day renchen, the king of Dongping, Cang, died.
translations.idiomatic: Jianchu 8, renchen in the first month of spring: Liu Cang, king of Dongping, died.

sentences.zh: 三月辛卯，葬東平憲王，賜鑾輅、龍旂。

translations.literal: In the third month, on the day xinmao, the accomplished king of Dongping was buried; he was granted the bell-carriage and dragon banners.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao in the third month King Xian of Dongping was buried with imperial hearse emblems.

sentences.zh: 夏六月，北匈奴大人率衆款塞降。

translations.literal: In summer, in the sixth month, a great man of the Northern Xiongnu led his multitude to knock at the passes and submit.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month of summer a Northern Xiongnu chief brought his people to surrender at the frontier.

sentences.zh: 冬十二月甲午，東巡狩，幸陳留、梁國、淮陽、潁陽。

translations.literal: In winter, in the twelfth month, on the day jiawu, he toured east on a hunt, favoring Chenliu, Liang, Huaiyang, and Yingyang.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu in the twelfth month of winter he began an eastern tour through Chenliu, Liang, Huaiyang, and Yingyang.

sentences.zh: 戊申，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On the day wushen, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On wushen the court returned to the palace.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：「五經剖判，去聖彌遠，章句遺辭，乖疑難正，恐先師微言將遂廢絕，非所以重稽古，求道真也。

translations.literal: An edict stated: "The Five Classics have been split apart; the distance from the sages grows ever farther; chapter-and-verse glosses leave phrases behind; doubts diverge and are hard to correct; I fear the subtle words of the former masters will then be abandoned and cut off—this is not the way to stress examining antiquity and seeking the true Way.
translations.idiomatic: An edict said: "The classics have fractured; we drift from the sages; rival glosses breed doubt; I fear the masters' subtle teachings will die if we do not recover antiquity."

sentences.zh: 其令羣儒選高才生，受學左氏、穀梁春秋、古文尚書、毛詩，以扶微學，廣異義焉。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that the host of Confucians select students of high talent to receive instruction in the Zuo, Guliang Spring and Autumn Annals, the old-text Documents, and the Mao Odes, in order to support subtle learning and broaden divergent meanings."
translations.idiomatic: Let scholars choose gifted pupils to study Zuo and Guliang, old-text Documents, and Mao Odes to keep minor traditions alive."

sentences.zh: 」 是歲，京師及郡國螟。

translations.literal: " That year, the capital region and the commanderies and principalities had crop worms.
translations.idiomatic: That year pests again hit the capital and provinces.

sentences.zh: 元和元年春正月，中山王焉來朝。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanhe, in spring, in the first month, the king of Zhongshan, Yan, came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 1, first month of spring: Prince Yan of Zhongshan attended court.

sentences.zh: 日南徼外蠻夷獻生犀、白雉。

translations.literal: Beyond the passes of Rinan, barbarian Yi presented a live rhinoceros and white pheasants.
translations.idiomatic: Rinan frontier tribes sent a live rhino and white pheasants.

sentences.zh: （劉欣明交州記曰：「犀，其毛如豕，蹏有三甲，頭如馬，有三角，鼻上角短，額上、頭上角長。

translations.literal: (Liu Xinming's Notes on Jiaozhou states: "The rhinoceros: its hair is like a pig's; its hooves have three plates; its head is like a horse's; it has three horns; the horn on the nose is short; those on the brow and crown are long.
translations.idiomatic: (A Jiaozhou note describes the rhinoceros: pig-like hair, three hooves, three horns.

sentences.zh: 」《異物志》曰：「角中特有光耀，白理如線，自本達末則爲通天犀。

translations.literal: " The Treatise on Strange Things states: "Inside the horn there is especially a gleam; white pattern like a thread—from root to tip it is the heaven-piercing rhinoceros."
translations.idiomatic: The Yiwu zhi adds that a "heaven-piercing" horn shows a bright line through it.

sentences.zh: 」） 閏月辛丑，濟陰王長薨。

translations.literal: ") In the intercalary month, on the day xinchou, the king of Jiyin, Chang, died.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month, on xinchou, King Chang of Jiyin died.

sentences.zh: 二月甲戌，詔曰：「王者八政，以食爲本，（尚書洪範八政，一曰食，是爲政本。

translations.literal: In the second month, on the day jiaxu, an edict stated: "The king's eight policies take food as root. (The Hong fan eight policies in the Documents: first is food—this is the root of government.)
translations.idiomatic: On jiaxu in the second month an edict said: "The eight policies of state put food first, as the Hong fan teaches."

sentences.zh: ）故古者急耕稼之業，致耒耜之勤，（耒耜，農器也。

translations.literal: Therefore in antiquity they stressed the enterprise of plowing and reaping, reaching the toil of plow and hoe. (Lei and si are farm tools.
translations.idiomatic: The ancients toiled at plow and hoe.

sentences.zh: 耒，其柄；

translations.literal: Lei is the handle;
translations.idiomatic: The handle is the lei;

sentences.zh: 耜，其刃。

translations.literal: si is the blade.)
translations.idiomatic: the blade is the si.)

sentences.zh: ）節用儲蓄，以備凶災，是以歲雖不登而人無飢色。

translations.literal: ) They economized and stored up to prepare against disaster; therefore although the year did not ripen, people had no hue of hunger.
translations.idiomatic: They saved grain against famine so that even in bad years folk did not go hungry.

sentences.zh: 自牛疫已來，穀食連少，良由吏敎未至，刺史、二千石不以爲負。

translations.literal: Since the cattle plague, grain has continuously been scarce—truly because clerical instruction has not reached them, and the regional inspectors and two thousand piculs do not take it as their burden.
translations.idiomatic: Since the cattle plague grain has stayed short because magistrates and governors have not taken it to heart.

sentences.zh: （負猶憂也。

translations.literal: (Fu means worry.)
translations.idiomatic: (Fu means "burden" or "worry."

sentences.zh: ）其令郡國募人無田欲徙它界就肥饒者，恣聽之。

translations.literal: ) Let it be ordered that commanderies and principalities recruit people without fields who wish to move to another border and go to rich soil—let them be heard as they will.
translations.idiomatic: Let every commandery recruit landless men willing to move to richer districts.

sentences.zh: 到在所，賜給公田，爲雇耕傭，賃種𩜋，（𩜋，糧也，古餉字，音式上反。

translations.literal: When they arrive where they are, grant them public fields, hire plowing labor for them, and lend seed and grain. (Xiang means grain; it is the ancient character for rations; its sound is shi-shang fan.)
translations.idiomatic: At their destination give them public land, hired plows, seed, and rations.

sentences.zh: ）貰與田器，勿收租五歲，除筭三年。

translations.literal: ) Lend them field tools; do not collect rent for five years; remit the poll tax reckoning for three years.
translations.idiomatic: Loan them tools; waive rent five years and poll tax three.

sentences.zh: 其後欲還本鄉者，勿禁。

translations.literal: If afterward they wish to return to their native villages, do not forbid them."
translations.idiomatic: Anyone who later wishes to go home may do so."

sentences.zh: 」 夏四月己卯，分東平國，封憲王蒼子尚爲任城王。

translations.literal: " In summer, in the fourth month, on the day jimao, Dongping state was divided and the accomplished king Cang's son Shang was enfeoffed as king of Rencheng.
translations.idiomatic: On jimao in the fourth month of summer Dongping was partitioned and Liu Shang, son of Liu Cang, became King of Rencheng.

sentences.zh: 六月辛酉，沛王輔薨。

translations.literal: On the day xinyou in the sixth month, the king of Pei, Fu, died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou in the sixth month King Fu of Pei died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月丁未，詔曰：「律云『掠者唯得榜、笞、立』。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the seventh month, on the day dingwei, an edict stated: "The statutes say: Those who interrogate may only use club, bamboo, and standing examination."
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei in the seventh month an edict quoted the code: interrogation may use only the rod, bamboo, and standing question.

sentences.zh: （《蒼頡篇》曰：「掠，問也。

translations.literal: (The Cangjie pian states: "Lue means question."
translations.idiomatic: (Lue means "to question."

sentences.zh: 」《廣雅》曰：「榜，擊也，音彭。

translations.literal: " The Guangya states: "Bang means strike; its sound is peng."
translations.idiomatic: Bang means to strike.

sentences.zh: 」《說文》曰：「笞，擊也。

translations.literal: " The Shuowen states: "Chi means strike."
translations.idiomatic: Chi means beating.

sentences.zh: 」立謂立而考訊之。

translations.literal: Li means standing while examining and questioning.)
translations.idiomatic: Li means questioning while standing.)

sentences.zh: ）又令丙，箠長短有數。

translations.literal: Moreover in ordinance Bing, bamboo lengths have fixed numbers.
translations.idiomatic: Ordinance Bing fixed bamboo-rod lengths for beating.

sentences.zh: （令丙爲篇之次也。

translations.literal: (Bing is the sequence of the ordinance section.)
translations.idiomatic: (Bing is a section title in the code.

sentences.zh: 《前書音義》曰：「令有先後，有令甲，令乙，令丙。

translations.literal: The Former Han Treatise on Sounds and Meanings states: "Ordinances have order; there is ordinance Jia, ordinance Yi, ordinance Bing."
translations.idiomatic: Han law was arranged in jia, yi, and bing sections.

sentences.zh: 」又景帝，京師定箠令，箠長五尺，本大一寸，其竹也末薄半寸，其平去節，故曰長短有數也。

translations.literal: " Moreover under Emperor Jing, in the capital the bamboo ordinance was fixed: bamboo five chi in length, one cun thick at the root, bamboo at the tip half a cun thin, flattened and nodes removed—therefore "lengths have numbers."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Di standardized the five-foot rod with set thickness.

sentences.zh: ）自徃者大獄已來，掠考多酷，鉆鑽之屬，（大獄謂楚王英等事也。

translations.literal: ) Since the great prison cases of former times, torture in examination has mostly been cruel—drills and augers and the like. (The great prison means cases such as the king of Chu, Ying, and others.
translations.idiomatic: Since the great witch-hunts, torture has grown cruel with drills and augers. (The "great prison" means cases like Prince Ying of Chu.

sentences.zh: 鉆音其廉反。

translations.literal: Zuan is read with the sound qi-lian fan.
translations.idiomatic: Read zuan qi-lian fan.

sentences.zh: 《說文》曰：「鉆，銸也。

translations.literal: The Shuowen states: "Zuan means pierce."
translations.idiomatic: Shuowen glosses zuan as pierce.

sentences.zh: 」《國語》曰：「中刑用鑽鑿。

translations.literal: The Discourses of the States states: "Middle punishments use drill and chisel."
translations.idiomatic: The Guoyu lists drill among middle punishments.

sentences.zh: 」皆謂慘酷其肌膚也。

translations.literal: " All mean cruelly lacerating the skin and flesh.)
translations.idiomatic: All refer to flaying the body.)

sentences.zh: ）慘苦無極。

translations.literal: Bitter cruelty without limit.
translations.idiomatic: Torture knew no limit.

sentences.zh: 念其痛毒，怵然動心。

translations.literal: Thinking of their pain and poison, I am startled and moved in the heart.
translations.idiomatic: Their agony wrings my heart.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰『鞭作官刑』，豈云若此？

translations.literal: The Documents states "the whip is made the punishment of functional officials"—can it mean like this?
translations.idiomatic: The Documents make the whip the official's tool—did they mean torture like this?

sentences.zh: （孔安國注《尚書》曰：「以鞭爲理官事之刑。

translations.literal: (Kong Anguo's commentary on the Documents states: "Use the whip as the punishment of administrative officials for their affairs."
translations.idiomatic: (Kong Anguo says the whip was for disciplining officials.

sentences.zh: 」）宜及秋冬理獄，明爲其禁。

translations.literal: ") It is fitting to handle prisons in autumn and winter and clearly make prohibitions for this."
translations.idiomatic: Review prisons in autumn and winter and publish clear bans."

sentences.zh: 」 八月甲子，太尉鄧彪罷，大司農鄭弘爲太尉。

translations.literal: " On the day jiazi in the eighth month, the grand commandant Deng Biao was dismissed; Zheng Hong, grand minister of agriculture, became grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi in the eighth month Deng Biao stepped down as grand commandant and Zheng Hong replaced him.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，詔曰：「朕道化不德，吏政失和，元元未諭，抵罪於下。

translations.literal: On the day guiyou, an edict stated: "My transforming influence lacks virtue; clerkly government misses harmony; the common people are not enlightened and bear guilt below.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou an edict said: "My rule lacks virtue; local government is out of tune; the people stumble into crime."

sentences.zh: 寇賊爭心不息，邊野邑屋不修。

translations.literal: Bandits and thieves contend in mind without ceasing; border wilds and town walls are not repaired.
translations.idiomatic: Robbery will not end; frontier hamlets and walls lie in disrepair.

sentences.zh: （「修」或作「充」。

translations.literal: (Some texts read "xiu" as "chong.")
translations.idiomatic: (Some manuscripts read "filled" instead of "repaired."

sentences.zh: ）永惟庶事，思稽厥衷，與凡百君子，共弘斯道。

translations.literal: ) Forever pondering the multitude of tasks, I wish to examine the center of them, together with the host of gentlemen, broadly to enlarge this Way.
translations.idiomatic: I ponder every task and would work with all good men to set things right.

sentences.zh: 中心悠悠，將何以寄？

translations.literal: In the center it is long and distant—on what shall I rely?
translations.idiomatic: My heart is heavy; where shall I turn?

sentences.zh: 其改建初九年爲元和元年。

translations.literal: Let Jianchu ninth year be changed to Yuanhe first year.
translations.idiomatic: Rename Jianchu 9 as Yuanhe 1.

sentences.zh: 郡國中都官繫囚減死一等，勿笞，詣邊縣；

translations.literal: Capital prisoners in the commanderies and principalities have death sentences reduced one grade, are not flogged, and go to border counties;
translations.idiomatic: Commute capital convicts one degree and send them to the frontier without the rod;

sentences.zh: 妻子自隨，占著在所。

translations.literal: wives and children follow of their own accord and register residence where they are.
translations.idiomatic: wives and children may follow and register there.

sentences.zh: 其犯殊死，一切募下蠶室；

translations.literal: As for those guilty of execution by dismemberment, in every case recruit them down to the silkworm chamber;
translations.idiomatic: Special capital crimes are commuted to castration;

sentences.zh: 其女子宮。

translations.literal: the women go to palace labor.
translations.idiomatic: women to palace labor.

sentences.zh: 繫囚鬼薪、白粲以上，皆減本罪一等，輸司寇作。

translations.literal: Prisoners at gui-xin and white-rice and upward—in every case their original crimes are reduced one grade and they are sent to minister-of-crimes labor.
translations.idiomatic: Gui-xin and white-rice convicts are cut one degree to minister-of-crimes corvee.

sentences.zh: 亡命者贖，各有差。

translations.literal: Fugitives from the law may ransom, each according to a graded scale."
translations.idiomatic: Fugitives may buy off sentences on a scale."

sentences.zh: 」 丁酉，南巡狩，詔所經道上，郡縣無得設儲跱。

translations.literal: " On the day dingyou, he toured south on a hunt; it was ordered that along the roads passed the commanderies and counties must not set up stored provisions and displays.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou he began a southern tour; counties along the route must not lay up feasts or displays.

sentences.zh: （儲，積也。

translations.literal: (Chu means accumulate.)
translations.idiomatic: (Chu means "stockpile."

sentences.zh: 跱，具也。

translations.literal: Zhi means complete provision.)
translations.idiomatic: Zhi means "full outfit."

sentences.zh: 言不預有蓄備。

translations.literal: It means not to prepare stores beforehand.)
translations.idiomatic: No pre-staged supplies.)

sentences.zh: ）命司空自將徒支柱橋梁。

translations.literal: It was ordered that the minister of works himself lead convict laborers to prop bridges and beams.
translations.idiomatic: The minister of works was to lead corvee gangs to shore up bridges.

sentences.zh: （柱音竹主反。

translations.literal: (Zhu is read with the sound zhu-zhu fan.)
translations.idiomatic: (Read zhu with the zhu initial and zhu rime for the word meaning "to prop.")

sentences.zh: ）有遣使奉迎、探知起居，二千石當坐。

translations.literal: ) If there are those who dispatch envoys to meet and escort or spy out comings and goings, the two thousand piculs shall sit for the crime.
translations.idiomatic: Any governor who sends escorts or spies will be punished.

sentences.zh: 其賜鰥、寡、孤、獨、不能自存者粟，人五斛。

translations.literal: Let there be granted to widowers, widows, orphans, the solitary, and those unable to support themselves grain, five hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: Give five hu of grain to each widower, widow, orphan, solitary person, and destitute soul.

sentences.zh: 九月乙未，東平王忠薨。

translations.literal: On the day yiwei in the ninth month, the king of Dongping, Zhong, died.
translations.idiomatic: On yiwei in the ninth month King Zhong of Dongping died.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，幸章陵，祠舊宅園廟，見宗室故人，賞賜各有差。

translations.literal: On the day xinchou, he favored Zhangling, sacrificed at the old dwelling garden temple, saw imperial kinsmen and old acquaintances, and granted gifts each according to a graded scale.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou he visited Zhangling, worshipped at the old Liu shrines, greeted kin and old friends, and gave graded gifts.

sentences.zh: 冬十月己未，進幸江陵，詔廬江太守祠南嶽，又詔長沙、零陵太守祠長沙定王、舂陵節侯、鬱林府君。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, on the day jiwei, he advanced in favor to Jiangling; it was ordered that the governor of Lujiang sacrifice to the southern marchmount, and moreover it was ordered that the governors of Changsha and Lingling sacrifice to the fixed king of Changsha, the marquis of Jieling at Chunling, and the lord of the prefecture of Yulin.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the tenth month he reached Jiangling, ordered Lujiang to sacrifice at the southern sacred peak, and Changsha and Lingling to honor Prince Ding of Changsha, Marquis Jie of Chunling, and the Yulin prefect.

sentences.zh: 還，幸宛。

translations.literal: Returning, he favored Wan.
translations.idiomatic: On the return journey he stopped at Wan.

sentences.zh: 十一月己丑，車駕還宮，賜從者各有差。

translations.literal: On the day jichou in the eleventh month, the imperial carriage returned to the palace; those who followed were granted gifts each according to a graded scale.
translations.idiomatic: On jichou in the eleventh month the court was home and followers rewarded by rank.

sentences.zh: 十二月壬子，詔曰：「《書》云：『父不慈，子不祗，兄不友，弟不恭，不相及也。

translations.literal: On the day renzi in the twelfth month, an edict stated: "The Documents states: If the father is not kind, the son is not reverent; if the elder brother is not friendly, the younger brother is not respectful—let them not implicate one another."
translations.idiomatic: On renzi in the twelfth month an edict quoted the Documents: kin should not punish one another for one man's fault.

sentences.zh: 』（祗，敬也。

translations.literal: (Zhi means reverent.)
translations.idiomatic: (Zhi means "reverent."

sentences.zh: 左傳胥臣云：「康誥曰：『父不慈，子不祗，兄不友，弟不恭，不相及也。

translations.literal: Zuo Zhuan, Xu Chen states: "The Kang announcement states: 'If the father is not kind, the son is not reverent; if the elder brother is not friendly, the younger brother is not respectful—let them not implicate one another.'"
translations.idiomatic: Zuo Zhuan cites the same Kang gao lines.

sentences.zh: 』」今康誥之言，事同而文異。

translations.literal: " Now the words of the Kang announcement are the same in matter but different in wording.)
translations.idiomatic: The received Kang gao text differs slightly from the quotation.

sentences.zh: ）徃者妖言大獄，所及廣遠，一人犯罪，禁至三屬，（即三族也。

translations.literal: ) In former times the great witchcraft cases reached far and wide; when one man committed a crime, prohibition extended to three kin-groups—that is, three clans.
translations.idiomatic: Past sorcery trials barred three whole clans from office.

sentences.zh: 謂父族、母族及妻族。

translations.literal: It means the father's clan, the mother's clan, and the wife's clan.
translations.idiomatic: Father's, mother's, and wife's kin were all banned.

sentences.zh: ）莫得垂纓仕宦王朝。

translations.literal: They could not hang tassel and serve in office at the royal court.
translations.idiomatic: None could take Han office.

sentences.zh: 如有賢才而沒齒無用，朕甚憐之，非所謂與之更始也。

translations.literal: If there were worthy talents who to the end of their teeth had no use, I pity them greatly—this is not what is called "giving them a fresh start."
translations.idiomatic: Good men grew old under disgrace—that was no fresh start.

sentences.zh: 諸以前妖惡禁錮者，一皆蠲除之，（《左傳》曰：「以重幣錮之。

translations.literal: As for all previously proscribed for sorcery and wickedness, in every case remit and remove the proscription. (The Zuo Zhuan states: "They restrained him with heavy bribes."
translations.idiomatic: Lift every ban imposed for those old sorcery cases. (Zuo uses "fetter" for political freeze.

sentences.zh: 」杜預注曰：「禁錮勿令仕也。

translations.literal: " Du Yu's commentary states: "Proscription means not letting them serve in office."
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu glosses proscription as barring office.

sentences.zh: 」）以明弃咎之路，但不得在宿衞而已。

translations.literal: ") In order to clarify the road of casting off blame—only they may not serve in night guard."
translations.idiomatic: Clear the way back to public life—only palace guard posts stay closed to them."

sentences.zh: 」 二年春正月乙酉，詔曰：「令云『人有產子者復，勿筭三歲』。

translations.literal: In the second year, in spring, in the first month, on the day yiyou, an edict stated: "The ordinance states: When a person bears a child, remit labor; do not reckon the poll tax for three years."
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 2, yiyou in the first month of spring: the code exempts new parents from corvée and poll tax for three years.

sentences.zh: 今諸懷𡜟者，（《說文》曰：「𡜟，孕也。

translations.literal: Now as for all who are with child. (The Shuowen states: "The graph meaning pregnancy means to be with child."
translations.idiomatic: For every woman now carrying a child (the Shuowen glosses the graph simply as "pregnant"),

sentences.zh: 」）賜胎養穀人三斛，復其夫，勿筭一歲，著以爲令。

translations.literal: ") grant grain for nourishing the fetus, three hu per person; remit their husbands' labor; do not reckon the poll tax for one year; write this into ordinance."
translations.idiomatic: grant three hu of grain to nourish the fetus; spare her husband corvée and poll tax for one year; and write this rule into the statutes."

sentences.zh: 」又詔三公曰：「方春生養，萬物莩甲，（《前書音義》曰：「莩，葉裏白皮也。

translations.literal: " Moreover he ordered the three dukes: "As spring nurtures life, the myriad things sprout sheaths. (The Former Han Treatise on Sounds and Meanings states: "Fu is the white skin inside the leaf."
translations.idiomatic: He told the three dukes: "Spring quickens life; buds split their sheaths.

sentences.zh: 」《易》曰「百果甲坼」也。

translations.literal: " The Changes states "the hundred fruits split their sheaths.")
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says the hundred fruits burst their husks."

sentences.zh: ）宜助萌陽，以育時物。

translations.literal: ) It is fitting to assist the budding yang and thereby nurture the seasonal creatures.
translations.idiomatic: Help the growing yang and nurture living things.

sentences.zh: 其令有司，罪非殊死且勿案驗，及吏人條書相告不得聽受，（條，事條也。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered to the responsible officials: for crimes that are not execution by dismemberment, for the time being do not examine cases; as for clerks and people submitting written accusations to one another, do not listen or accept. (Tiao means itemized document.)
translations.idiomatic: Let no office try non-capital cases for now; reject mutual denunciations on petty written charges.

sentences.zh: ）兾以息事寧人，敬奉天氣。

translations.literal: ) Hoping thereby to still affairs and quiet the people, reverently to comply with Heaven's qi.
translations.idiomatic: So may affairs quiet and the people match Heaven's mood.

sentences.zh: 立秋如故。

translations.literal: At Establishment of Autumn, as before.
translations.idiomatic: At Beginning of Autumn resume normal justice.

sentences.zh: 夫俗吏矯飾外貌，似是而非，揆之人事則恱耳，論之陰陽則傷化，朕甚饜之，甚苦之。

translations.literal: As for vulgar clerks who put on a false outward show, seeming yes but really no—applied to human affairs they please the ear; judged by yin and yang they harm transformation—I am utterly weary of them, utterly distressed by them.
translations.idiomatic: Village clerks who look honest and lie—pleasant to hear yet ruinous to good government—disgust and weary me.

sentences.zh: 安靜之吏，悃愊無華，（《說文》云：「悃愊，至誠也。

translations.literal: As for quiet and calm clerks, sincere and honest without ornament. (The Shuowen states: "Kun-bi means utmost sincerity."
translations.idiomatic: Quiet magistrates who are plainspoken and free of show (Shuowen glosses kun-bi as "utterly sincere."

sentences.zh: 」悃音苦本反。

translations.literal: Kun is read with the sound ku-ben fan.)
translations.idiomatic: Read kun ku-ben fan.

sentences.zh: 愊音孚逼反。

translations.literal: Bi is read with the sound fu-bi fan.)
translations.idiomatic: Read bi fu-bi fan.)

sentences.zh: ）日計不足，月計有餘。

translations.literal: By day their reckoning seems insufficient; by month they have surplus.
translations.idiomatic: They look slow by the day yet leave a surplus by the month.

sentences.zh: （莊子曰：「有庚桑子者，偏得老聃之道，以居㟪壘之山。

translations.literal: (Zhuangzi states: "There was Gengsang Chu, who had partially obtained Laozi's Way, and dwelt on Mount Wei-lei.
translations.idiomatic: (Zhuangzi tells of Gengsang Chu on Mount Wei-lei.

sentences.zh: 㟪壘之人相與云：『庚桑子之始來，吾洒然異之；

translations.literal: The people of Wei-lei said to one another: 'When Gengsang Chu first came, we were startled and thought him strange;
translations.idiomatic: The locals first thought him strange;

sentences.zh: 今吾日計之不足，歲計之有餘，庶幾其聖人乎？

translations.literal: now when we reckon him by the day he seems insufficient, when we reckon him by the year he has surplus—perhaps he is a sage?"
translations.idiomatic: day by day he seemed idle, year by year he left plenty—perhaps a sage?"

sentences.zh: 』」）如襄城令劉方，（方字伯況，平原人。

translations.literal: ") Such as the magistrate of Xiangcheng, Liu Fang. (Fang's style was Bokuang; he was a man of Pingyuan.)
translations.idiomatic: Liu Fang of Xiangcheng was such a man. (Style Bokuang, from Pingyuan.

sentences.zh: ）吏人同聲謂之不煩，雖未有它異，斯亦殆近之矣。

translations.literal: ) Clerks and people with one voice called him "not troublesome"; although there were no other unusual marks, this also was nearly close to it.
translations.idiomatic: His staff called him "no bother"; though he won no fame, he came near this ideal.

sentences.zh: 閒勑二千石各尚寬明，而今富姦行賂於下，貪吏枉法於上，使有罪不論而無過被刑，甚大逆也。

translations.literal: From time to time I admonish those of two thousand piculs each to honor leniency and clarity; yet now the rich commit bribery below while greedy clerks bend the law above, causing the guilty not to be judged while the guiltless suffer punishment—this is greatly contrary to right.
translations.idiomatic: I keep telling governors to be clear and mild, yet rich men bribe clerks, clerks twist the law, the guilty walk free and innocents are beaten—that is a gross perversion.

sentences.zh: 夫以苛爲察，以刻爲明，以輕爲德，以重爲威，四者或興，則下有怨心。

translations.literal: To take harshness as scrutiny, to take cruelty as clarity, to take light punishment as virtue, to take heavy punishment as awe—if any of these four arise, then below there will be hearts of resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Harshness masquerading as diligence, cruelty as clarity, light sentences as kindness, heavy ones as terror—any of these breeds popular resentment.

sentences.zh: 吾詔書數下，冠蓋接道，而吏不加理，人或失職，其咎安在？

translations.literal: My edicts have gone down repeatedly; caps and canopies crowd the roads, yet clerks do not become more orderly—some lose their duties; where does the fault lie?
translations.idiomatic: Edicts stream out and couriers crowd the post roads, yet local rule does not improve—whose fault is that?

sentences.zh: 勉思舊令，稱朕意焉。

translations.literal: Strive to ponder the old ordinances and accord with my intent."
translations.idiomatic: Study the old statutes again and match my will."

sentences.zh: 」 二月甲寅，始用四分歷。

translations.literal: " In the second month, on the day jiayin, for the first time the quarter-remainder calendar was used.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the second month the court adopted the quarter-day calendar.

sentences.zh: （《續漢書》曰：「時待詔張盛、京房、鮑業等以四分歷請與待詔楊岑等共課歲餘，盛等所中多，四分之歷始頗施行。

translations.literal: (The Continued Han Documents states: "At the time the awaiting edicts Zhang Sheng, Jing Fang, Bao Ye and others, with the quarter-remainder calendar, requested jointly with the awaiting edicts Yang Cen and others to compare for more than a year; Sheng and others hit the mark more often; the quarter-remainder calendar then began to be somewhat put in practice."
translations.idiomatic: (The Xu Han shu records a trial of calendrical systems: Zhang Sheng's quarter-day method won and was adopted.

sentences.zh: 」） 詔曰：「今山川鬼神應典禮者，尚未咸秩。

translations.literal: ") An edict stated: "Now mountains, rivers, and ghosts that answer to canonical rites are not yet all given ordered sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: An edict said: "Spirits entitled to rite have not all received ordered worship."

sentences.zh: （咸，皆也。

translations.literal: (Xian means all.
translations.idiomatic: (Xian means "all."

sentences.zh: 秩，序也。

translations.literal: Zhi means order.)
translations.idiomatic: Zhi means "proper sequence."

sentences.zh: 言山川之神尚未次序而祭之。

translations.literal: It means that the spirits of mountains and rivers have not yet been given sequence and sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: Many river and peak gods still lack their turn at the altar.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：「咸秩無文。

translations.literal: The Documents states: "All give order even to the unpatterned."
translations.idiomatic: The Documents say to put even unsung spirits in their place.

sentences.zh: 」）其議增修羣祀，以祈豐年。

translations.literal: ") Let there be deliberation on increasing and repairing the host of sacrifices, in order to pray for a plentiful year."
translations.idiomatic: Expand and repair the pantheon of sacrifices to pray for good harvests."

sentences.zh: 」 丙辰，東巡狩。

translations.literal: " On the day bingchen, he toured east on a hunt.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen he set out on an eastern tour.

sentences.zh: 己未，鳳皇集肥城。

translations.literal: On the day jiwei, phoenixes gathered at Feicheng.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei phoenixes were reported at Feicheng.

sentences.zh: （肥城，縣名，屬太山郡，故城在今濟州平陰縣東南。

translations.literal: (Feicheng is a county name belonging to Taishan commandery; the old city is now southeast of Pingyin county in Jizhou.)
translations.idiomatic: (Feicheng lay in Taishan, southeast of modern Pingyin.

sentences.zh: ）乙丑，帝耕於定陶。

translations.literal: On the day yichou, the emperor plowed at Dingtao.
translations.idiomatic: On yichou he turned the sacred furrow at Dingtao.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：「三老，尊年也。

translations.literal: An edict stated: "The three elders honor age;
translations.idiomatic: An edict said: "The three elders honor age;

sentences.zh: 孝悌，淑行也。

translations.literal: filial piety and fraternal duty are virtuous conduct;
translations.idiomatic: filial piety and brotherly love are virtue;

sentences.zh: 力田，勤勞也。

translations.idiomatic: Envoys sacrificed to Yao at Chengyang's Spirit Terrace.

translations.idiomatic: (Chengyang county was in Jiyin.

translations.idiomatic: Folk also call it the Great Mother's Spirit Mound."

sentences.zh: 」）辛未，幸太山，柴告岱宗。

translations.idiomatic: On xinwei he climbed Tai and performed the wood-fire offering to the peak.

translations.literal: There were thirty yellow swans that came from the southwest, passed over the sacrifice mound, and went northeast past the palace roofs, soaring, rising and descending.
translations.idiomatic: Thirty yellow swans flew from the southwest over the altar and northeast past the hall.

sentences.zh: 進幸奉高。

translations.literal: He advanced in favor to Fenggao.
translations.idiomatic: He continued to Fenggao.

translations.idiomatic: On renshen he worshipped the Five Thearchs at the Bright Hall on the Wen River.

sentences.zh: （《前書》曰：「濟南人公玉帶上黃帝時明堂圖，中有一殿，四面無壁，以茅蓋，通水，水圜宮垣爲複道；

translations.literal: (The Former Han states: "A man of Jinan, Gongyu Dai, presented a chart of the Bright Hall of the Yellow Thearch's time: in the center was one hall, four sides without walls, thatched with rushes; water ran through; water encircled the palace wall as double passage;
translations.idiomatic: (Former Han says Gongyu Dai's chart showed a thatched hall with water channels.

sentences.zh: 上有樓，從西南入，名曰崐崘，以拜祀上帝。

translations.literal: above there was a tower; entry from the southwest was called Kunlun, for bowing in sacrifice to High God.
translations.idiomatic: A tower opened from the southwest called Kunlun for worship of Shangdi.

sentences.zh: 於是上作明堂於汶上，如帶圖焉。

translations.literal: Thereupon the sovereign built a Bright Hall above the Wen as in Dai's chart."
translations.idiomatic: The Han hall on the Wen followed that plan."

sentences.zh: 」汶水出太山朱虛縣萊蕪山。

translations.literal: The Wen River rises from Mount Laiwu in Zhu county of Tai.
translations.idiomatic: The Wen rises on Mount Laiwu.

sentences.zh: ）癸酉，告祠二祖、四宗，（二祖謂高祖、世祖。

translations.literal: ") On the day guiyou, he announced sacrifice to the two ancestors and four exalted cults.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou he reported the sacrifice to the two founding ancestors and four temple exalted.

sentences.zh: 四宗謂文帝爲太宗，武帝爲世宗，宣帝爲中宗，明帝爲顯宗。

translations.literal: (The two ancestors mean Gaozu and Shizu; the four exalted mean Emperor Wen as Tai-zong, Emperor Wu as Shi-zong, Emperor Xuan as Zhong-zong, and Emperor Ming as Xian-zong.)
translations.idiomatic: (The two ancestors are Gaozu and Guangwu; the four exalted are Wen, Wu, Xuan, and Ming.

sentences.zh: ）大會外內羣臣。

translations.literal: He greatly assembled inner and outer hosts of ministers.
translations.idiomatic: He held a great assembly of the whole court.

sentences.zh: 丙子，詔曰：「朕巡狩岱宗，柴望山川，告祀明堂，以章先勳。

translations.literal: On the day bingzi, an edict stated: "I have toured and hunted Dai Zong, performed chai and gazed on mountains and rivers, announced sacrifice at the Bright Hall, thereby to manifest the former meritorious deeds.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi he said: "I have sacrificed on Tai, announced the peaks, worshipped at the Bright Hall, to show forth our founders' merit."

sentences.zh: 其二王之後，（《禮記》曰：「存二王之後，尊賢不過二代。

translations.literal: As for the descendants of the two kings. (The Record of Rites states: "Preserve the descendants of the two kings; honoring the worthy does not pass two generations."
translations.idiomatic: The heirs of the two former dynasties (the Rites preserve Yin and Zhou descendants.

sentences.zh: 」《公羊傳》曰：「存二王之後，所以通三正也。

translations.literal: The Gongyang Commentary states: "Preserve the descendants of the two kings—thereby the three correct beginnings are linked."
translations.idiomatic: Gongyang says this links the three calendar beginnings.

sentences.zh: 」漢之二王，殷、周之後也。

translations.literal: " The two kings of Han are the descendants of Yin and Zhou.)
translations.idiomatic: Han enfeoffed the houses of Shang and Zhou.

sentences.zh: ）先聖之胤，（《東觀記》曰：「孔子後襃成侯等咸來助祭。

translations.idiomatic: Descendants of Confucius came to the rites. (The Dongguan ji names the Baocheng marquels.

sentences.zh: 」）東后蕃衞，（東后謂東方國君也。

translations.literal: ") The lords of the east as fence and screen. (The lords of the east mean the rulers of eastern states;
translations.idiomatic: Eastern lords as the royal screen (feudatories guard the throne.

translations.literal: the feudatories are fence and screen for the Son of Heaven; therefore they are called fence and screen.)
translations.idiomatic: Hence they are called the royal hedge.

translations.idiomatic: Uncles, brothers, sons, and grandsons (from the Documents, "Lu xing."

translations.idiomatic: All are imperial Liu princes ranked by generation.

translations.idiomatic: Ministers, imperial kin, and the yao and huang outer zones

translations.idiomatic: yao lay two thousand li from the capital, huang two thousand five hundred;

translations.idiomatic: yao can still be taught by rites;

translations.literal: huang means they are wild and unsettled.)

translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan glosses: bless the worthy with rank and salary;

translations.idiomatic: heavenly bodies stand in true alignment.

translations.idiomatic: On renchen he went to Dongping and sacrificed at King Xian's tomb.

translations.literal: (The tomb is now east of Xuchang county in Yunzhou.)
translations.idiomatic: (The tomb lay east of Xuchang.

translations.literal: (The dowager was Emperor Yuan's Lady Fu Zhaoyi.)
translations.idiomatic: (She was Lady Fu, honored consort of Emperor Yuan.

translations.idiomatic: King Gong of Dingtao was Liu Kang; his tomb stood north of Jiyin.)

translations.idiomatic: (The pass lay south of Jincheng on the Taihang ridge, near three springs.

translations.idiomatic: On yisi in the fourth month of summer a guest star entered the Purple Palace asterism.

translations.idiomatic: On yimao the court returned to the capital.

translations.idiomatic: Mi is the father's temple.)

translations.idiomatic: The Book of Changes speaks of the king "arriving at the temple."

translations.idiomatic: He reported his journey at Gaozu's temple.

translations.idiomatic: It has a fowl body, red tail, and five-colored plumage.
