translations.idiomatic: The Fengshan shu sets one ox and ten shi of wine per hundred households.

sentences.zh: 」臣賢案：此女子百戶，若是戶頭之妻，不得更稱爲戶；

translations.literal: Your servant Xian considers: if these women of one hundred households were heads of households' wives, they could not again be called households;
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian argues these are female-headed households.

sentences.zh: 此謂女戶頭，即今之女戶也。

translations.literal: this means female heads of household—that is, present-day female-headed households.
translations.idiomatic: That matches the female-household register.

sentences.zh: 天下稱慶，恩當普洽，所以男戶賜爵，女子賜牛酒。

translations.literal: The empire celebrates; grace ought to spread everywhere; therefore men's households receive rank and women receive ox and wine.
translations.idiomatic: The whole realm rejoices, so men get rank and women ox and wine.

sentences.zh: ）令天下大酺五日。

translations.literal: Let the empire hold a general drinking feast for five days.
translations.idiomatic: Order five days of public feasting.

sentences.zh: 賜公卿已下錢帛各有差；

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the dukes and ministers downward cash and silk each according to a graded scale;
translations.idiomatic: Give dukes and ministers graded cash and silk;

sentences.zh: 及洛陽人當酺者布，戶一匹，城外三戶共一匹。

translations.literal: and to the people of Luoyang who should feast, cloth, one bolt per household, outside the walls three households sharing one bolt.
translations.idiomatic: Luoyang residents at the feast get one bolt per in-wall household, one bolt per three out-wall households.

sentences.zh: 賜博士員弟子見在太學者布，人三匹。

translations.literal: Let there be granted to erudite disciples who are present in the Grand Academy cloth, three bolts per person.
translations.idiomatic: Academy students in residence receive three bolts each.

sentences.zh: 令郡國上明經者，口十萬以上五人，不滿十萬三人。

translations.literal: Let commanderies and principalities memorialize those who understand the classics: above one hundred thousand mouths, five men; below one hundred thousand, three men."
translations.idiomatic: Each commandery shall send five classicists per hundred thousand people, three if under that count."

sentences.zh: 」 改廬江爲六安國，江陵復爲南郡。

translations.literal: " Lujiang was changed to be the state of Lu'an; Jiangling was again made Nan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Lujiang became the kingdom of Lu'an; Jiangling was restored as Nan commandery.

sentences.zh: （建初四年改爲江陵國，今又復之。

translations.literal: (In the fourth year of Jianchu it had been changed to Jiangling state; now it is restored.)
translations.idiomatic: (Jiangling had been a kingdom since Jianchu 4; that was revoked.

sentences.zh: ）徙江陵王恭爲六安王。

translations.literal: The king of Jiangling, Gong, was moved to be king of Lu'an.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Gong of Jiangling was transferred to Lu'an.

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚子，詔曰：「春秋於春每月書『王』者，重三正，慎三微也。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the seventh month, on the day gengzi, an edict stated: "The Spring and Autumn Annals in spring each month writes 'king'—it stresses the three correct beginnings and is careful about the three subtle beginnings."
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the seventh month of autumn an edict discussed why the classic writes "king" each spring month: the three beginnings and three "subtle" months.

sentences.zh: （三正謂天、地、人之正。

translations.literal: (The three correct mean the correct beginnings of Heaven, Earth, and Man.)
translations.idiomatic: (Heaven, earth, and human calendar beginnings.

sentences.zh: 所以有三者，由有三微之月，王者所當奉而成之。

translations.literal: The reason there are three is that there are months of three subtle beginnings, which the king ought to honor and complete.
translations.idiomatic: The three "micro" months begin the three calendars.

sentences.zh: 禮緯曰：「正朔三而改，文質再而復。

translations.literal: The Ritual weft states: "First months change thrice; pattern and substance alternate twice."
translations.idiomatic: A ritual weft says the first month shifts with dynasty.

sentences.zh: 三微者，三正之始，萬物皆微，物色不同，故王者取法焉。

translations.literal: The three subtle beginnings are the starts of the three correct months; the myriad things are all subtle; their colors differ; therefore the king takes them as model.
translations.idiomatic: In each the world is still "small"; colors differ; the king takes them as models.

sentences.zh: 十一月，時陽氣始施於黃泉之下，色皆赤。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, when yang qi first spreads below the Yellow Springs, the colors are all red.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month yang stirs below ground; all is tinged red.

sentences.zh: 赤者陽氣，故周爲天正，色尚赤。

translations.literal: Red is yang qi; therefore Zhou took Heaven's correct month and honored red.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou used that month as Heaven's first and honored red.

sentences.zh: 十二月，萬物始牙而色白。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, the myriad things first sprout teeth and the color is white.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month things sprout white.

sentences.zh: 白者陰氣，故殷爲地正，色尚白。

translations.literal: White is yin qi; therefore Yin took Earth's correct month and honored white.
translations.idiomatic: Yin took it as Earth's first and honored white.

sentences.zh: 十三月，萬物莩甲而出，其色皆黑，人得加功展業，故夏爲人正，色尚黑。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth month, the myriad things burst sheaths and emerge; their colors are all black; people can add work and expand enterprise; therefore Xia took Man's correct month and honored black."
translations.idiomatic: The "thirteenth" month of Xia saw black shoots and human labor; Xia honored black."

sentences.zh: 」尚書大傳曰：「夏十三月爲正，平旦爲朔。

translations.literal: The Great Commentary on the Documents states: "Xia took the thirteenth month as correct, cockcrow as new moon.
translations.idiomatic: The Documents commentary lists Xia, Yin, and Zhou new-year rules.

sentences.zh: 殷以十二月爲正，雞鳴爲朔。

translations.literal: Yin took the twelfth month as correct, cockcrow as new moon.
translations.idiomatic: Yin used midnight-to-cockcrow reckoning.

sentences.zh: 周以十一月爲正，夜半爲朔。

translations.literal: Zhou took the eleventh month as correct, midnight as new moon."
translations.idiomatic: Zhou began the year at midnight of the eleventh month."

sentences.zh: 」必以三微之月爲正者，當爾之時，物皆尚微，王者受命，當扶微理弱，奉成之義也。

translations.literal: The necessity of taking the three subtle months as correct is that at that time things are still subtle; when the king receives the Mandate, he ought to support the subtle and regulate the weak—this is the meaning of honoring completion.)
translations.idiomatic: The king must "finish" the tender year by gentle rule.

sentences.zh: ）律十二月立春，不以報囚。

translations.literal: ) In the statutes, the twelfth month has Establishment of Spring; do not use it to report on prisoners.
translations.idiomatic: Statute: do not pass final sentence in the month that contains Beginning of Spring.

sentences.zh: （報猶論也。

translations.literal: (Bao means judge.)
translations.idiomatic: (Bao means "to judge."

sentences.zh: 立春陽氣至，可以施生，故不論囚。

translations.literal: At Establishment of Spring yang qi arrives; one may apply nurture to life; therefore one does not judge prisoners.)
translations.idiomatic: Spring nurtures life, so courts pause executions.

sentences.zh: ）月令冬至之後，有順陽助生之文，（月令仲冬：「是月也，日短至，陰陽爭，諸生蕩，君子身欲寧，事欲靜，以待陰陽之所定也。

translations.literal: ) After the Monthly Ordinances' winter solstice, there is language of complying with yang and assisting life. (The Monthly Ordinances, mid-winter: "In this month the day is shortest; yin and yang contend; the myriad living things quiver; the gentleman's person desires quiet, affairs desire stillness, to await what yin and yang fix."
translations.idiomatic: The winter section of the Monthly Ordinances urges quiet until yin and yang settle.

sentences.zh: 」）而無鞠獄斷刑之政。

translations.literal: ") Yet there is no policy of trying prisons and deciding punishments.
translations.idiomatic: Yet it does not authorize criminal trials in that season.

sentences.zh: 朕咨訪儒雅，稽之典籍，以爲王者生殺，宜順時氣。

translations.literal: I have consulted Confucians and examined the canons; I consider that the king's granting and taking life ought to comply with seasonal qi.
translations.idiomatic: I have asked the scholars: life and death should follow the seasons.

sentences.zh: 其定律，無以十一月、十二月報囚。

translations.literal: Fix it in law: do not report on prisoners in the eleventh or twelfth month."
translations.idiomatic: Therefore write into law: no capital case reports in the eleventh or twelfth month."

sentences.zh: 」 九月壬辰，詔：「鳳皇、黃龍所見亭部無出二年租賦。

translations.literal: " In the ninth month, on the day renchen, an edict: "In the hamlets where phoenixes and yellow dragons appeared, remit no rent or levies for two years.
translations.idiomatic: On renchen in the ninth month: two years' tax holiday for hamlets where phoenixes or dragons appeared.

sentences.zh: （《東觀記》曰：「鳳皇見肥城句窳亭槐樹上。

translations.literal: (The Eastern Lodge Record states: "A phoenix appeared on a locust tree at Gouyu pavilion in Feicheng."
translations.idiomatic: (A phoenix sat on a Feicheng locust.

sentences.zh: 」《古今注》云：「黃龍見洛陽元延亭部。

translations.literal: " The Notes Ancient and Modern states: "A yellow dragon appeared in the Yuan-yan hamlet district of Luoyang."
translations.idiomatic: A dragon appeared in Luoyang's Yuan-yan district.

sentences.zh: 」窳音庾。

translations.literal: " Yu is read yu.)
translations.idiomatic: Read the place name with yu rime.)

sentences.zh: ）加賜男子爵，人二級；

translations.literal: ) Add grant to men of the empire noble ranks, two grades per person;
translations.idiomatic: Add two noble steps for every man;

sentences.zh: 先見者帛二十匹，近者三匹，太守三十匹，令、長十五匹，丞、尉半之。

translations.literal: to those who saw them first, twenty bolts of silk; to the near, three bolts; to the grand administrator, thirty bolts; to magistrate and chief, fifteen bolts; to assistant and commandant, half.
translations.idiomatic: first witnesses twenty bolts of silk, neighbors three, governors thirty, magistrates fifteen, assistants half.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：『雖無德與汝，式歌且舞。

translations.literal: The Odes states: 'Although I have no virtue to give you, we use song and dance.'
translations.idiomatic: The Odes say, "Though I lack gifts for you, we may still sing and dance."

sentences.zh: 』（詩小雅也。

translations.literal: ' (Minor Odes.)
translations.idiomatic: (Minor Odes.

sentences.zh: 取雖無大德，要有喜恱之心，欲歌舞也。

translations.literal: It takes the meaning that although there is no great virtue, there must be a heart of joy and pleasure, desiring song and dance.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss: even small joy deserves celebration.

sentences.zh: 式，用也。

translations.literal: Shi means use.)
translations.idiomatic: Shi means "therefore."

sentences.zh: ）它如賜爵故事。

translations.literal: Other matters follow the precedent of granting rank."
translations.idiomatic: Other gifts follow the usual rank-reward rules.

sentences.zh: 」 丙申，徵濟南王康、中山王焉會烝祭。

translations.literal: " On the day bingshen, the kings Kang of Jinan and Yan of Zhongshan were summoned to meet at the winter offering.
translations.idiomatic: On bingshen Princes Kang and Yan were summoned for the winter sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月壬辰，日南至，初閉關梁。

translations.literal: In winter, in the eleventh month, on the day renchen, the sun reached its southernmost; for the first time passes and bridges were closed.
translations.idiomatic: On renchen at the winter solstice the court first closed frontier passes and bridges.

sentences.zh: （《易》曰：「先王以至日閉關，商旅不行。

translations.literal: (The Changes states: "The former kings closed the passes on the solstice days; merchants and travelers did not travel."
translations.idiomatic: (The Changes says kings shut passes at the solstices.

sentences.zh: 」王弼注曰：「冬至陰之復，夏至陽之復，故爲復即至於寂然大靜，先王則天地而行者也。

translations.literal: " Wang Bi's commentary states: "Winter solstice is yin's return; summer solstice is yang's return; therefore for fu, reaching utmost great quiet, the former kings modeled Heaven and earth in acting."
translations.idiomatic: Wang Bi explains rest at the turning of yin and yang.

sentences.zh: 」） 三年春正月乙酉，詔曰：「蓋君人者，視民如父母，有憯怛之憂，有忠和之敎，匍匐之救。

translations.literal: ") In the third year, in spring, in the first month, on the day yiyou, an edict stated: "In general one who rules the people looks on the people as father and mother; there is anxious grief, there is loyal and harmonious teaching, there is crawling rescue.
translations.idiomatic: Yuanhe 3, yiyou in the first month of spring: a ruler should love the people as parents, teach loyalty and harmony, and crawl to save them in need.

sentences.zh: （《周禮》：「鄉司徒以鄉三物敎萬民，一曰六德，謂智、仁、聖、義、忠、和。

translations.literal: (The Zhou Rituals: "The minister of education of the district uses three things to teach the myriad people: first the six virtues, meaning wisdom, benevolence, sageliness, righteousness, loyalty, and harmony."
translations.idiomatic: (The Zhou li lists wisdom, benevolence, sageliness, righteousness, loyalty, and harmony.

sentences.zh: 」詩鄁風曰：「凡民有喪，匍匐救之。

translations.literal: " The "Bei feng" of the Odes states: "Whenever the people have a funeral, crawl to save them."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes urge crawling to help neighbors in mourning.

sentences.zh: 」）其嬰兒無父母親屬，及有子不能養食者，稟給如律。

translations.literal: ") As for infants without parents or kin, and those who have children but cannot feed and nourish them, supply rations according to statute."
translations.idiomatic: Feed abandoned infants and families who cannot feed children, as the statutes provide."

sentences.zh: 」 丙申，北巡狩，濟南王康、中山王焉、西平王羨、六安王恭、樂成王黨、淮陽王昞、任城王尚、沛王定皆從。

translations.literal: " On the day bingshen, he toured north on a hunt; the kings Kang of Jinan, Yan of Zhongshan, Xian of Xiping, Gong of Lu'an, Dang of Lecheng, Bing of Huaiyang, Shang of Rencheng, and Ding of Pei all followed.
translations.idiomatic: On bingshen he began a northern tour accompanied by seven princes.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，帝耕于懷。

translations.literal: On the day xinchou, the emperor plowed at Huai.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou he plowed the sacred field at Huai.

sentences.zh: 二月壬寅，告常山、魏郡、清河、鉅鹿、平原、東平郡太守、相曰：「朕惟巡狩之制，以宣聲敎，考同遐迩，解釋怨結也。

translations.literal: In the second month, on the day renyin, he addressed the grand administrators and ministers of Changshan, Wei, Qinghe, Julu, Pingyuan, and Dongping commanderies, saying: "I reflect that the system of tour and hunt is to spread sound and teaching, examine near and far alike, and loosen knotted resentment.
translations.idiomatic: On renyin in the second month he wrote the northern governors: touring spreads instruction and clears popular grievance.

sentences.zh: 今『四國無政，不用其良』，（詩小雅曰：「日月告凶，不用其行。

translations.literal: Now "the four states lack good government; they do not use their good men." (The "Minor Odes" states: "Sun and moon announce ill omens; they do not use their proper courses.
translations.idiomatic: He quoted the Odes: "The four states lack good government; they do not use their good men."

sentences.zh: 四國無政，不用其良。

translations.literal: The four states lack good government; they do not use their good men."
translations.idiomatic: The lines blame misrule on ignoring worthies.

sentences.zh: 」言四方之國無政者，由天子不用善人也。

translations.literal: " It means that the states of the four quarters lack good government because the Son of Heaven does not use good men.)
translations.idiomatic: The gloss blames the throne when the regions fail.

sentences.zh: ）駕言出游，欲親知其劇易。

translations.literal: I raise the carriage and speak of traveling, desiring personally to know their hardship or ease.
translations.idiomatic: I ride out to see whether life is hard or easy there.

sentences.zh: 前祠園陵，遂望祀華、霍，（華、霍，山名也。

translations.literal: Previously I sacrificed at the mausoleum parks; then I performed distant sacrifice to Hua and Huo. (Hua and Huo are mountain names.
translations.idiomatic: I have worshipped the tombs and offered distant sacrifice to Mount Hua and Mount Huo.

sentences.zh: 在今廬江灊縣西南，亦名天柱山。

translations.literal: They are now southwest of Qian county in Lujiang, also called Mount Tianzhu.)
translations.idiomatic: (Here "Huo" is the sacred peak southwest of Qian in Lujiang, also called Tianzhu.

sentences.zh: 《爾雅》曰華山爲西嶽，霍山爲南嶽。

translations.literal: The Erya states that Mount Hua is the western marchmount and Mount Huo the southern marchmount.)
translations.idiomatic: The Erya names Hua west and Huo south—here Huo is the southern peak honored on this tour.

sentences.zh: ）東祡岱宗，爲人祈福。

translations.literal: ) Eastward I performed chai on Dai Zong, seeking blessing for the people.
translations.idiomatic: Eastward I sacrificed on Tai for the people's blessing.

sentences.zh: 今將禮常山，遂徂北土，歷魏郡，經平原，升踐隄防，詢訪耆老，咸曰『徃者汴門未作，深者成淵，淺則泥塗』。

translations.literal: Now I shall perform rites at Mount Chang, then go on to the northern lands, pass through Wei, cross Pingyuan, climb dikes and levees, inquire of the aged—all say: "In former times before the Bian sluice was built, the deep became abyss, the shallow mud."
translations.idiomatic: Now I go to worship Changshan, then north through Wei and Pingyuan; elders recall that before the Bian gates were built the river was chasm or mudflat.

sentences.zh: 追惟先帝勤人之德，（謂永平十二年修汴渠。

translations.literal: I trace the late emperor's virtue of toiling for the people. (It refers to repairing the Bian canal in the twelfth year of Yongping.)
translations.idiomatic: I recall Emperor Ming's Yongping 12 work on the Bian canal.

sentences.zh: ）厎績遠圖，復禹弘業，（《尚書》曰：「覃懷厎績。

translations.literal: ) He set down achievement and planned far. (The Documents states: "Tan and Huai he set down achievement."
translations.idiomatic: He laid a lasting foundation, renewing Yu's work. (The Documents praise "setting down achievement."

sentences.zh: 」孔安國注云：「厎，置；

translations.literal: " Kong Anguo's commentary states: "Di means establish;
translations.idiomatic: Kong Anguo glosses di as "set down."

sentences.zh: 績，功也。

translations.literal: ji means merit."
translations.idiomatic: Ji is "merit."

sentences.zh: 」遠圖猶長筭也。

translations.literal: " Yuan tu means long reckoning.)
translations.idiomatic: Yuan tu is long-term planning.

sentences.zh: 言能復禹爲理水之大功。

translations.literal: It speaks of being able to renew Yu's great merit in ordering water.)
translations.idiomatic: It means completing Yu's flood control.

sentences.zh: ）聖跡滂流，至于海表。

translations.literal: ) Sage traces spread broadly, reaching to beyond the seas.
translations.idiomatic: That work reached the sea.

sentences.zh: 不克堂構，朕甚慙焉。

translations.literal: I am not able to complete hall and roof beam; I am greatly ashamed.
translations.idiomatic: I cannot match my father's "hall and roof"; I am ashamed.

sentences.zh: （《尚書》曰：「若考作室，旣厎法，厥子乃不肯堂，矧肯構。

translations.literal: (The Documents states: "If the father builds a house, having set down the plan, his son then is unwilling to complete the hall—how much less the roof beam."
translations.idiomatic: (The Documents image of the son who cannot finish his father's house.

sentences.zh: 」）月令，孟春善相丘陵土地所宜。

translations.literal: ") The Monthly Ordinances: in the first month of spring, skillfully inspect hills and mounds for what land suits.
translations.idiomatic: The Monthly Ordinances tell the first month to survey land for crops.

sentences.zh: （月令：「孟春之月，善相丘陵、阪險、原隰土地所宜，五穀所殖，以敎導人，必躬親之，田事旣飭。

translations.literal: (The Monthly Ordinances: "In the first month of spring, skillfully inspect hills, slopes, plains, and low wet ground for what land suits, what the five grains may grow in, in order to teach and guide the people; you must personally attend; field work is then set in order."
translations.idiomatic: The first month's chapter orders the ruler to walk the fields himself.

sentences.zh: 」）今肥田尚多，未有墾闢。

translations.literal: ") Now rich fields are still many and have not been opened for cultivation.
translations.idiomatic: Good land still lies fallow.

sentences.zh: 其悉以賦貧民，給與糧種，務盡地力，勿令游手。

translations.literal: Let them all be assigned to the poor; give grain and seed; strive to exhaust the land's power; do not allow idlers.
translations.idiomatic: Assign it to the poor with seed grain; put every acre to work; ban idlers.

sentences.zh: 所過縣邑，聽半入今年田租，以勸農夫之勞。

translations.literal: In the counties and cities passed through, allow half payment of this year's field rent, in order to encourage the farmers' labor.
translations.idiomatic: Counties along the route may collect only half this year's land tax to reward the farmers.

sentences.zh: 」 乙丑，勑侍御史、司空曰：「方春，所過無得有所伐殺。

translations.literal: " On the day yichou, he ordered the attendant censors and the minister of works: "It is spring; along the way passed there must be no cutting or killing.
translations.idiomatic: On yichou he told the censorate and minister of works: in spring the tour must kill no living thing.

sentences.zh: 車可以引避，引避之；

translations.literal: Where the carriage can yield the way, yield the way;
translations.idiomatic: Where the train can detour around trees or game, it must detour;

sentences.zh: 騑馬可輟解，輟解之。

translations.literal: Where the outer horses can be unhitched, unhitch them;
translations.idiomatic: Unhitch the outer team horses wherever possible;

sentences.zh: （夾轅者爲服馬，服馬外爲騑馬。

translations.literal: (Those flanking the pole are the team horses; outside the team horses are the outer horses.)
translations.idiomatic: (The pole team are "service" horses; the wing horses are the outer pair.

sentences.zh: ）《詩》云：『敦彼行葦，牛羊勿踐履。

translations.literal: ) The Odes states: 'Thick grow the rushes in the row; let not cattle or sheep trample them.'
translations.idiomatic: The Odes say, "Thick stand the rushes by the road; let no herd trample them."

sentences.zh: 』（詩大雅云。

translations.literal: ' (The Major Odes so states.)
translations.idiomatic: (From the Major Odes.

sentences.zh: 鄭玄注云：「敦敦然道旁之葦，牧牛羊者無使踐履折傷之，況於人乎！

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan's commentary explains: "So thick stand the rushes by the road—herders of cattle and sheep must not let them trample and break them—how much more so with people!"
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan: protect the roadside rushes from herds—how much more must we spare people!

sentences.zh: 」）禮，人君伐一草木不時，謂之不孝。

translations.literal: ") By ritual, if the lord of men fells one tree or plant out of season, it is called unfilial.
translations.idiomatic: Ritual calls it unfilial for a ruler to cut one plant out of season.

sentences.zh: （禮記孔子曰：「伐一樹，殺一獸，不以其時，非孝也。

translations.literal: (The Record of Rites, Confucius states: "To fell one tree, kill one beast, not in its season—is not filial."
translations.idiomatic: (The Li ji quotes Confucius: killing or cutting out of season is unfilial.

sentences.zh: 」）俗知順人，莫知順天。

translations.literal: ") The vulgar know how to comply with men; they do not know how to comply with Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Common folk obey other men but not Heaven.

sentences.zh: 其明稱朕意。

translations.literal: Make clear and proclaim my intent."
translations.idiomatic: Publish this clearly."

sentences.zh: 」 戊辰，進幸中山，遣使者祠北嶽，出長城。

translations.literal: " On the day wuchen, he advanced in favor to Zhongshan, sent envoys to sacrifice to the northern marchmount, and went out through the Long Wall.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen he reached Zhongshan, sacrificed at Mount Heng, and rode beyond the Wall.

sentences.zh: （史記，蒙恬爲秦築長城，西自臨洮，東至海。

translations.literal: (The Records: Meng Tian for Qin built the Long Wall, west from Lintao, east to the sea.)
translations.idiomatic: (Shiji: Meng Tian's Qin wall ran from Lintao to the sea.

sentences.zh: ）癸酉，還幸元氏，祠光武、顯宗於縣舍正堂；

translations.literal: On the day guiyou, returning, he favored Yuanshi and sacrificed to Guangwu and Xianzong in the main hall of the county headquarters;
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou he stopped at Yuanshi and worshipped Guangwu and Ming in the county hall;

sentences.zh: 明日又祠顯宗于始生堂，（明帝生于常山元氏傳舍也。

translations.literal: the next day he again sacrificed to Xianzong in the Hall of First Birth. (Emperor Ming was born at the Yuanshi relay inn in Changshan.)
translations.idiomatic: next day he offered again at the hall where Ming was born (the Yuanshi posthouse.

sentences.zh: ）皆奏樂。

translations.literal: In both cases music was performed.
translations.idiomatic: Both services had music.

sentences.zh: 三月丙子，詔高邑令祠光武於即位壇。

translations.literal: In the third month, on the day bingzi, it was ordered that the magistrate of Gaoyi sacrifice to Guangwu at the accession mound.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi in the third month the Gaoyi magistrate sacrificed to Guangwu at the enthronement altar.

sentences.zh: 復元氏七年傜役。

translations.literal: Yuanshi's seven years of corvee labor were remitted.
translations.idiomatic: Yuanshi was excused seven years of corvee.

sentences.zh: 己卯，進幸趙。

translations.literal: On the day jimao, he advanced in favor to Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: On jimao he traveled to Zhao.

sentences.zh: 庚辰，祠房山於靈壽。

translations.literal: On the day gengchen, he sacrificed to Mount Fang in Lingshou.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen he worshipped Mount Fang at Lingshou.

sentences.zh: （靈壽，縣名，屬常山郡，今恒州縣也。

translations.literal: (Lingshou is a county name belonging to Changshan commandery; it is now in Heng prefecture.)
translations.idiomatic: (Lingshou was in Changshan.

sentences.zh: 房山在今恒州房山縣西北，俗名王母山，上有王母祠。

translations.literal: Mount Fang is now northwest of Fangshan county in Heng prefecture, vulgarly called Queen Mother Mountain; above it is a Queen Mother shrine.)
translations.idiomatic: Mount Fang is northwest of Fangshan, folk-named Queen Mother peak.

sentences.zh: ）辛卯，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On the day xinmao, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao the court returned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 賜從行者各有差。

translations.literal: Those who followed were granted gifts each according to a graded scale.
translations.idiomatic: Followers received graded gifts.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙寅，太尉鄭弘免，大司農宋由爲太尉。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day bingyin, the grand commandant Zheng Hong was dismissed; Song You, grand minister of agriculture, became grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin in the fourth month Zheng Hong left office and Song You became grand commandant.

sentences.zh: （由字叔路，長安人。

translations.literal: (You's style was Shulu; he was a man of Chang'an.)
translations.idiomatic: (Song You, style Shulu, from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: ） 五月丙子，司空弟五倫罷，太僕袁安爲司空。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, on the day bingzi, the minister of works Di Wu Lun was dismissed; Yuan An, grand coachman, became minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi in the fifth month Di Wu Lun was dismissed as minister of works and Yuan An appointed.

sentences.zh: 秋八月乙丑，幸安邑，觀鹽池。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the eighth month, on the day yichou, he favored Anyi and inspected the salt pools.
translations.idiomatic: On yichou in the eighth month he visited Anyi and the salt lagoons.

sentences.zh: （許慎云：「河東鹽池，袤五十一里，廣七里，周百一十六里。

translations.literal: (Xu Shen states: "The Hedong salt pool measures fifty-one li east-west, seven li broad, one hundred sixteen li around."
translations.idiomatic: (Xu Shen measured the Hedong pool at fifty-one by seven li.

sentences.zh: 」今蒲州虞鄉縣西。

translations.literal: " It is now west of Yuxiang county in Pu prefecture.)
translations.idiomatic: It lay west of modern Yongji in Shanxi.

sentences.zh: ）九月，至自安邑。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, he arrived, coming from Anyi.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month he returned from Anyi.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，北海王基薨。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, the king of Beihai, Ji, died.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter King Ji of Beihai died.

sentences.zh: 燒當羌叛，寇隴西。

translations.literal: The Shao Dang Qiang rebelled and raided Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: The Shao Dang Qiang rose and struck Longxi.

sentences.zh: 是歲，西域長史班超擊斬疏勒王。

translations.literal: That year, the chief clerk of the Western Regions Ban Chao attacked and beheaded the king of Su-le.
translations.idiomatic: That year Western Regions chief clerk Ban Chao executed the king of Su-le.

sentences.zh: 章和元年春三月，護羌校尉傅育追擊叛羌，戰歿。

translations.literal: In the first year of Zhanghe, in spring, in the third month, the colonel director of the Qiang, Fu Yu, pursued and attacked the rebel Qiang and died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: Zhanghe 1, third month of spring: Fu Yu, colonel director of the Qiang, pursued rebels and fell in battle.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙子，令郡國中都官繫囚減死一等，詣金城戍。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day bingzi, it was ordered that capital prisoners in the commanderies and principalities have death sentences reduced one grade and go to garrison Jin cheng.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi in the fourth month capital convicts were commuted one degree and sent to Jin cheng.

sentences.zh: 六月戊辰，司徒桓虞免。

translations.literal: On the day wuchen in the sixth month, the minister of education Huan Yu was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen in the sixth month Minister of Education Huan Yu was removed.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，司空袁安爲司徒，光祿勳任隗爲司空。

translations.literal: On the day guimao, Minister of Works Yuan An became minister of education, and Ren Kui, superintendent of the imperial household, became minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao Yuan An became minister of education and Ren Kui minister of works.

sentences.zh: （桓虞字仲春，馮翊萬年人。

translations.literal: (Huan Yu's style was Zhongchun; he was a man of Wannian in Fengyi.
translations.idiomatic: (Huan Yu, style Zhongchun, from Wannian.

sentences.zh: 隗字仲和，南陽宛人。

translations.literal: Kui's style was Zhonghe; he was a man of Wan in Nanyang.)
translations.idiomatic: Ren Kui, style Zhonghe, from Wan.

sentences.zh: ） 秋七月癸卯，齊王晃有罪，貶爲蕪湖侯。

translations.literal: ) In autumn, in the seventh month, on the day guimao, the king of Qi, Huang, having committed a crime, was degraded to be marquis of Wuhu.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao in the seventh month Prince Huang of Qi was demoted to Marquis of Wuhu for a crime.

sentences.zh: （蕪湖，縣名，屬丹陽，故城在今宣州當塗縣東南。

translations.literal: (Wuhu is a county name belonging to Danyang; the old city is now southeast of Dangtu county in Xuan prefecture.)
translations.idiomatic: (Wuhu lay in Danyang, southeast of Dangtu.

sentences.zh: ）壬子，淮陽王昞薨。

translations.literal: On the day renzi, the king of Huaiyang, Bing, died.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi King Bing of Huaiyang died.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑擊破北單于，斬之。

translations.literal: The Xianbei attacked and defeated the Northern Shanyu and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbei routed the Northern Shanyu and killed him.

sentences.zh: 燒當羌寇金城，護羌校尉劉盱討之，斬其渠帥。

translations.literal: The Shao Dang Qiang raided Jin cheng; Liu Xu, colonel director of the Qiang, attacked them and beheaded their chieftain.
translations.idiomatic: Shao Dang Qiang struck Jin cheng; Liu Xu destroyed them and beheaded their leader.

sentences.zh: 壬戌，詔曰：「朕聞明君之德，啟迪鴻化，緝熙康乂，光照六幽，（緝熙，光明也。

translations.literal: On the day renxu, an edict stated: "I have heard that the enlightened lord's virtue opens great transformation, gathers and brightens peace and order, and his light shines into the six darknesses. (Ji-xi means bright light;
translations.idiomatic: On renxu an edict praised the enlightened ruler's light reaching every hidden corner. (Ji-xi means radiance;

sentences.zh: 六幽謂六合幽隱之處也。

translations.literal: the six darknesses mean the hidden places of the six coordinates.)
translations.idiomatic: the six "dark" regions are the six directions.

sentences.zh: ）訖惟人靣，靡不率俾，仁風翔于海表，威霆行乎鬼區。

translations.literal: Extending to every human face, none fails to obey; humane wind soars beyond the seas, awesome thunder walks in the ghost regions.
translations.idiomatic: All mankind obeys; humane breeze crosses the seas and terror strikes the ghost lands.

sentences.zh: （鬼區即鬼方。

translations.literal: (The ghost regions are Gui Fang.)
translations.idiomatic: (Ghost regions means distant Gui Fang.

sentences.zh: ）然後敬恭明祀，膺五福之慶，獲來儀之貺。

translations.literal: Then he reverently performs bright sacrifice and receives the blessing of the five felicities, obtains the gift of the coming phoenix.
translations.idiomatic: Then he sacrifices and receives the five blessings and the phoenix's gift.

sentences.zh: （尚書五福：一曰壽，二曰富，三曰康寧，四曰攸好德，五曰考終命。

translations.literal: (The Documents' five felicities: first long life; second wealth; third health and peace; fourth love of virtue; fifth a natural end.
translations.idiomatic: (The Documents list five felicities: long life, wealth, peace, love of virtue, and a good death.

sentences.zh: 來儀謂鳳也。

translations.literal: Lai yi means the phoenix.
translations.idiomatic: Lai yi means the phoenix.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：「鳳皇來儀。

translations.literal: The Documents states: "The phoenixes come in ordered beauty."
translations.idiomatic: The Documents say, "The phoenixes come in ritual grace."

sentences.zh: 」）朕以不德，受祖宗弘烈。

translations.literal: ") I, lacking in virtue, have received the great blazing work of ancestors and fathers.
translations.idiomatic: I, unworthy, have inherited my ancestors' great enterprise.

sentences.zh: 乃者鳳皇仍集，麒麟並臻，甘露宵降，嘉穀滋生，芝草之類，歲月不絕。

translations.literal: Recently phoenixes have repeatedly gathered, qilin have arrived together, sweet dew has fallen at night, auspicious grain has sprouted and grown; lingzhi and the like have not ceased month after month.
translations.idiomatic: Lately phoenixes flock, qilin come, dew falls at night, grain sprouts with omens, lingzhi never stops.

sentences.zh: 朕夙夜祗畏上天，無以彰于先功。

translations.literal: Morning and night I revere High Heaven; I have no means to manifest the former merit.
translations.idiomatic: I tremble before Heaven yet cannot display my fathers' merit.

sentences.zh: 今改元和四年爲章和元年。

translations.literal: Now let Yuanhe fourth year be changed to Zhanghe first year.
translations.idiomatic: Rename Yuanhe 4 as Zhanghe 1.

sentences.zh: 」 秋，令是月養衰老，授几杖，行糜粥飲食。

translations.literal: " In autumn, it was ordered that in this month they nurture the aged and failing, grant staffs and stools, and distribute gruel and drink. (The order of mid-autumn in the Monthly Ordinances.)
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, per the Monthly Ordinances mid-autumn chapter, the court ordered care for the aged: staffs, gruel, and porridge.

sentences.zh: （月令仲秋之令。

translations.literal: (The Monthly Ordinances, mid-autumn month.)
translations.idiomatic: (Mid-autumn ordinances.

sentences.zh: ）其賜高年二人共布帛各一匹，以爲醴酪。

translations.literal: ) Let there be granted to the very aged two persons sharing one bolt of cloth and silk each, for making sweet and sour milk foods.
translations.idiomatic: Give each pair of elders one bolt for dairy foods.

sentences.zh: 死罪囚犯法在丙子赦前而後捕繫者，皆減死，勿笞，詣金城戍。

translations.literal: Capital prisoners whose offenses were committed before the bingzi amnesty but who were seized afterward—all have death reduced, are not flogged, and go to garrison Jin cheng.
translations.idiomatic: Death-row prisoners whose crimes predated the bingzi amnesty but who were arrested later are commuted and sent to Jin cheng without the rod.

sentences.zh: 八月癸酉，南巡狩。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, on the day guiyou, he toured south on a hunt.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou in the eighth month he began a southern tour.

sentences.zh: 壬午，遣使者祠昭靈后於小黃園。

translations.literal: On the day renwu, he sent envoys to sacrifice to Empress Zhao Ling at the Lesser Huang garden.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu envoys sacrificed to Lady Zhao Ling at Lesser Huang.

sentences.zh: （小黃，縣，屬陳留郡，故城在今汴州陳留縣東北。

translations.literal: (Lesser Huang is a county belonging to Chenliu commandery; the old city is now northeast of Chenliu county in Bian prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: (Lesser Huang was northeast of Chenliu.

sentences.zh: 漢舊儀曰：「昭靈后，高祖母，起兵時死小黃北，後爲作園廟于小黃柵。

translations.literal: Han Old Rites states: "Empress Zhao Ling was Gaozu's mother; she died north of Lesser Huang when troops rose; afterward a garden shrine was made at Lesser Huang fence."
translations.idiomatic: Han ritual texts say Gaozu's mother died north of Lesser Huang when the army rose; a shrine was built there.

sentences.zh: 」陳留風俗傳曰：「沛公起兵野戰，喪皇妣于黃鄉。

translations.literal: " Chenliu Customs states: "When Duke of Pei raised troops in field battle, he lost his imperial mother at Huang township.
translations.idiomatic: Chenliu lore adds that the Duke of Pei lost his mother at Huang in the wars.

sentences.zh: 天下平定。

translations.literal: When the empire was pacified,
translations.idiomatic: When peace returned,

sentences.zh: 乃使使者以梓宮招魂幽野，於是丹蛇在水，自洒濯之，入于梓宮，其浴處有遺髮，故謚曰昭靈夫人。

translations.literal: he then sent envoys with the catalpa coffin to summon the soul in the dark wilds; thereupon a red snake was in the water, washed itself, and entered the catalpa coffin; at the bathing place there was hair left behind; therefore her posthumous title was Lady Zhao Ling."
translations.idiomatic: he summoned her soul with a coffin; a red snake bathed and entered the coffin, leaving hair—hence the title Lady Zhao Ling."

sentences.zh: 」）甲申，徵任城王尚會睢陽。

translations.literal: ") On the day jiashen, the king of Rencheng, Shang, was summoned to meet at Suiyang.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen Prince Shang of Rencheng was ordered to Suiyang.

sentences.zh: 戊子，幸梁。

translations.literal: On the day wuzi, he favored Liang.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi he visited Liang.

sentences.zh: 己丑，遣使祠沛高原廟，豐枌榆社。

translations.literal: On the day jichou, he sent envoys to sacrifice at Pei's lofty temple to Gaozu and at the Fen and white elm community altars of Pei.
translations.idiomatic: On jichou envoys worshipped Gaozu's Pei temple and the Feng white-elms earth altars.

sentences.zh: （《前書音義》曰：「枌，白榆。

translations.literal: (The Former Han Treatise on Sounds and Meanings states: "Fen means white elm.
translations.idiomatic: (Fen is the white elm.

sentences.zh: 高祖里社在豐縣東北十五里。

translations.literal: Gaozu's village community is fifteen li northeast of Feng county."
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu's village altar lay fifteen li northeast of Feng.

sentences.zh: 」原廟，解見光武紀。

translations.literal: " The lofty temples—explanation appears in the annals of Guangwu.)
translations.idiomatic: The "lofty" shrines are glossed under Guangwu.

sentences.zh: ）乙未，幸沛，祠獻王陵，徵會東海王政。

translations.literal: On the day yiwei, he favored Pei, sacrificed at the tomb of King Xian, and summoned a meeting with the king of Donghai, Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: On yiwei he reached Pei, sacrificed at King Xian's tomb, and summoned Prince Zheng of Donghai.

sentences.zh: 乙未晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On the last day of the month yiwei, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: At month's end on yiwei the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 九月庚子，幸彭城，東海王政、沛王定、任城王尚皆從。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, on the day gengzi, he favored Pengcheng; the king of Donghai, Zheng, the king of Pei, Ding, and the king of Rencheng, Shang, all followed.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the ninth month he was at Pengcheng with Princes Zheng, Ding, and Shang.

sentences.zh: 辛亥，幸壽春。

translations.literal: On the day xinhai, he favored Shouchun.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai he visited Shouchun.

sentences.zh: 壬子，詔郡國中都官繫囚減死罪一等，詣金城戍；

translations.literal: On the day renzi, it was ordered that capital prisoners in the commanderies and principalities have death crimes reduced one grade and go to garrison Jin cheng;
translations.idiomatic: On renzi capital convicts were commuted one degree to Jin cheng;

sentences.zh: 犯殊死者，一切募下蠶室；

translations.literal: those guilty of execution by dismemberment, in every case recruit them down to the silkworm chamber;
translations.idiomatic: special capital crimes were castrated;

sentences.zh: 其女子宮；

translations.literal: the women go to palace labor.
translations.idiomatic: women to palace labor.

sentences.zh: 繫囚鬼薪、白粲已上，減罪一等，輸司寇作。

translations.literal: Prisoners at gui-xin and white-rice and upward have crimes reduced one grade and are sent to minister-of-crimes labor.
translations.idiomatic: Gui-xin and white-rice convicts dropped one degree to minister-of-crimes corvee.

sentences.zh: 亡命者贖：死罪縑二十匹，右趾至髡鉗城旦舂七匹，完城旦至司寇三匹；

translations.literal: Fugitives from the law may ransom: for death, twenty bolts of fine silk; for right-toe amputation down through shaved-head wall-dawn labor, seven bolts; for intact wall-dawn labor down to minister-of-crimes labor, three bolts;
translations.idiomatic: Fugitives: twenty bolts for death, seven for mutilation sentences, three for lesser labor;

sentences.zh: 吏民犯罪未發覺，詔書到自告者，半入贖。

translations.literal: for clerks and commoners whose crimes have not yet been discovered, those who confess themselves when the edict arrives pay half the ransom.
translations.idiomatic: self-surrender when the edict arrives halves the ransom.

sentences.zh: 復封阜陵侯延爲阜陵王。

translations.literal: The marquis of Fuling, Yan, was again enfeoffed as king of Fuling.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Yan of Fuling was restored to his kingship.

sentences.zh: 己未，幸汝陰。

translations.literal: On the day jiwei, he favored Ru-yin.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei he visited Ru-yin.

sentences.zh: （縣名，屬汝南郡，今潁川縣。

translations.literal: (It is a county name belonging to Runan commandery; now Yingchuan county.)
translations.idiomatic: (Ru-yin was in Runan, modern Yingzhou area.

sentences.zh: ）冬十月丙子，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, on the day bingzi, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi in the tenth month of winter the court returned home.

sentences.zh: 北匈奴屋蘭儲等率衆降。

translations.literal: Wu-lan-chu and others of the Northern Xiongnu led their multitude in surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Wu-lan-chu of the Northern Xiongnu surrendered with his people.

sentences.zh: 是歲，西域長史班超擊莎車，大破之。

translations.literal: That year, the chief clerk of the Western Regions Ban Chao attacked Shache and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: That year Ban Chao crushed the kingdom of Shache (Yarkand).

sentences.zh: 月氏國遣使獻扶拔、師子。

translations.literal: The state of Yuezhi sent envoys to present fu-ba and lions.
translations.idiomatic: Yuezhi sent envoys with fu-ba beasts and lions.

sentences.zh: （扶拔，似麟無角。

translations.literal: (Fu-ba resembles the qilin but has no horns.
translations.idiomatic: (Fu-ba looks like a hornless qilin.

sentences.zh: 拔音步末反。

translations.literal: Ba is read with the sound bu-mo fan.)
translations.idiomatic: Read ba bu-mo fan.

sentences.zh: ） 二年春正月，濟南王康、阜陵王延、中山王焉來朝。

translations.literal: ") In the second year, in spring, in the first month, the kings Kang of Jinan, Yan of Fuling, and Yan of Zhongshan came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Zhanghe 2, first month of spring: Princes Kang, Yan of Fuling, and Yan of Zhongshan attended court.

sentences.zh: 壬辰、帝崩於章德前殿，年三十三。

translations.literal: On the day renchen, the emperor died in the front hall of Zhangde, aged thirty-three sui.
translations.idiomatic: On renchen the emperor died in the Zhangde front hall at thirty-three.

sentences.zh: 遺詔無起寑廟，一如先帝法制。

translations.literal: His testamentary edict ordered that no resting temple be raised—entirely according to the late emperors' regulations.
translations.idiomatic: His will forbade a separate temple, as for his predecessors.

sentences.zh: 論曰：魏文帝稱「明帝察察，章帝長者」。

translations.literal: The assessment states: Wei Wendi called Emperor Ming "scrutinizing" and Emperor Zhang "an elder."
translations.idiomatic: The historian notes: Cao Pi called Ming "over-sharp" and Zhang "the elder type."

sentences.zh: （以上華嶠之辭。

translations.literal: (The above is Hua Qiao's wording.)
translations.idiomatic: (Hua Qiao's judgment.

sentences.zh: ）章帝素知人厭明帝苛切，事從寬厚。

translations.literal: ) Emperor Zhang from the first knew that people detested Emperor Ming's harsh severity; affairs followed breadth and kindness.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang knew the people had tired of Ming's harshness and leaned toward lenience.

sentences.zh: 感陳寵之義，除慘獄之科。

translations.literal: Moved by Chen Chong's righteousness, he abolished the statutes on cruel prisons.
translations.idiomatic: Heeded Chen Chong and repealed cruel-prison statutes.

sentences.zh: （寵時爲尚書，以吏政嚴切，乃上書除慘酷之科五十餘條，具本傳也。

translations.literal: (At the time Chong was master of writing; because clerkly government was harshly tight, he submitted a memorial abolishing more than fifty articles of cruel severity—the full account is in his basic biography.)
translations.idiomatic: (Chen Chong as master of writing had memorialized away fifty cruel provisions—see his biography.

sentences.zh: ）深元元之愛，著胎養之令。

translations.literal: ) He deeply enacted love for the common people and established the ordinance on nurturing the fetus.
translations.idiomatic: He deepened care for commoners and issued the fetal-nourishment edict.

sentences.zh: （元和二年令，諸懷姙者賜穀，人三斛。

translations.literal: (The ordinance of the second year of Yuanhe granted grain to all who were pregnant, three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: (The Yuanhe 2 edict had given three hu of grain to every pregnant woman.

sentences.zh: ）奉承明德太后，盡心孝道。

translations.literal: ) He received and served Empress Dowager Mingde with full heart in filial devotion.
translations.idiomatic: He served Empress Dowager Mingde with complete filial devotion.

sentences.zh: 割裂名都，以崇建周親。

translations.literal: He carved apart famous commanderies in order to honor and establish closest kin.
translations.idiomatic: He carved up great fiefs to enrich his nearest kin.

sentences.zh: （周，至也。

translations.literal: (Zhou means utmost.)
translations.idiomatic: (Zhou here means "closest" or "most intimate.")

sentences.zh: ）平傜簡賦，而人賴其慶。

translations.literal: ) He leveled corvée and simplified taxes, and the people relied on his blessings.
translations.idiomatic: He lightened corvée and cut taxes so the people lived under his blessing.

sentences.zh: 又體之以忠恕，文之以禮樂。

translations.literal: Moreover he embodied them with loyalty and forgiveness and patterned them with ritual and music.
translations.idiomatic: He tempered rule with loyalty and forbearance, and adorned it with ritual and music.

sentences.zh: 故乃蕃輔克諧，羣后德讓。

translations.literal: Therefore the feudal assistants achieved harmony; the host of lords yielded in virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Feudatories fell into harmony and princes deferred to one another in virtue.

sentences.zh: 謂之長者，不亦宜乎！

translations.literal: To call him an elder—was it not fitting!
translations.idiomatic: Small wonder they called him an elder.

sentences.zh: 在位十三年，郡國所上符瑞，合於圖書者數百千所。

translations.literal: In the thirteen years of his reign, the tallies and omens submitted by commanderies and principalities that matched the charts and writings numbered in the hundreds and thousands of instances.
translations.idiomatic: In thirteen years the provinces reported hundreds upon hundreds of omens matching the apocryphal charts.

sentences.zh: 烏呼懋哉！

translations.literal: Alas, how splendid!
translations.idiomatic: Ah, how splendid!

sentences.zh: （懋，美也。

translations.literal: (Mao means beautiful.)
translations.idiomatic: (Mao means "splendid."

sentences.zh: ） 贊曰：肅宗濟濟，天性愷悌。

translations.literal: ) The encomium states: Suzong stately and grand, by nature kind and easy.
translations.idiomatic: The verse praise runs: "Suzong stood grave and tall, kind and easy by nature.

sentences.zh: 於穆后德，諒惟淵體。

translations.literal: Ah, grave the virtue of the empress; truly deep was his substance.
translations.idiomatic: Deep ran the queenly virtue within him, a truly profound nature.

sentences.zh: （於穆，歎美也。

translations.literal: (Yu mu is sighing praise.
translations.idiomatic: (Yu mu is a cry of admiration.

sentences.zh: 《尚書》曰「齊聖廣淵」也。

translations.literal: The Documents states "acute, sage, broad, and deep."
translations.idiomatic: The Documents praise the sage as acute, holy, wide, and deep.

sentences.zh: ）左右蓺文，斟酌律禮。

translations.literal: ) At his left and right he patterned culture, weighing statutes and ritual.
translations.idiomatic: At his side stood culture and learning, balanced against law and rite.

sentences.zh: （蓺文謂諸儒講五經同異，帝親稱制論決也。

translations.literal: (Patterned culture means the various Confucians expounding agreements and differences in the Five Classics, the emperor personally pronouncing the rescript and deciding.)
translations.idiomatic: (The classics conference at White Tiger Hall, where he ruled from the chair.

sentences.zh: 律謂詔云「立春不以報囚」也。

translations.literal: Statutes mean the edict stating "at Establishment of Spring do not report on prisoners."
translations.idiomatic: Statutes: the spring edict halting executions.

sentences.zh: 禮謂修禘祫，登靈臺之屬。

translations.literal: Rites mean repairing the di and xia, ascending the Spirit Terrace, and the like.)
translations.idiomatic: Rites: the grand di and xia, the Spirit Terrace, and such observances.

sentences.zh: ）思服帝道，弘此長懋。

translations.literal: He pondered and submitted to the Way of the emperors; he enlarged this lasting excellence.
translations.idiomatic: He pondered the imperial Way and built lasting excellence.

sentences.zh: 儒館獻歌，戎亭虛候。

translations.literal: The academy presented songs; frontier posts stood empty of alert.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars sang his praises at the academy; frontier beacons rarely fired.

sentences.zh: （獻歌謂崔駰游太學時上四巡等頌。

translations.literal: (Presenting songs means when Cui Yin toured the Grand Academy he submitted odes such as the four tours.)
translations.idiomatic: (Cui Yin at the Grand Academy had offered the "Four Tours" and similar odes.)

sentences.zh: ）氣調時豫，憲平人富。

translations.literal: ) Qi was attuned and the seasons content; statutes were even and the people rich.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven and the seasons stayed in tune; the laws ran fair and the people grew wealthy.
